TUNGO
Documentation›Introduction

Tungo Design System

The single source of truth for how Tungo looks and reads — colors, type, spacing and components, documented as tokens you can ship.

Overview

What's inside

Three foundations carry the whole product. Each is its own page with live specimens and copy-paste tokens.

Colors
Brand ramps, semantic states, neutrals, surfaces & charts.
Typography
Inter, an 11-step scale, weights and product roles.
Spacing & radius
A 4-point spacing scale and the corner-radius set.
Principles

How the system thinks

  • One job per token. Mint is the action, magenta is the brand, each semantic color owns one meaning. Discipline is what keeps the calm look.
  • Hierarchy from size, weight and space — not from piling on color. Color marks meaning; structure does the rest.
  • Dark by default, light when needed. Every token has a value for both modes, so nothing breaks when the canvas flips.
  • Accessible floor. Contrast is measured, meaning never rides on color alone, and body text never drops below 14px.

Installation

Drop the tokens into any project. They're plain CSS custom properties — framework-optional.

Step 1

Load the fonts

Tungo runs on Inter, with JetBrains Mono for code. Add the link in your document head.

<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700;800&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
Step 2

Add the tokens

Paste the color and type custom properties into your global stylesheet, then reference them everywhere. The full set lives on the Design tokens page.

:root{
  --color-cta:#11EE92;       /* buttons */
  --color-primary:#FF1E9A;   /* brand   */
  --font-sans:'Inter',system-ui,sans-serif;
  --text-md:16px;            /* body    */
}
body{font-family:var(--font-sans);font-size:var(--text-md)}

Colors

The Tungo brand palette extended into a full product system: scaled brand ramps, semantic states, warm neutrals, surfaces and a chart series.

What this adds

From brand sheet to product system

The brand colors stay exactly as specified. These are the missing pieces a product needs the day it ships.

Error / Danger
Was missing — added
Destructive actions, failed states, validation
Warning
Was missing — added
Caution, quota limits, "are you sure" moments
Tint ramps ×6
Each hue, 11 steps
Light fills, hovers, borders, dark fills
Warm neutral ramp
Extended to 12 steps
Borders, dividers, 3 text levels, surfaces
Core ramps

Brand hues, fully scaled

Each brand color now spans 50 → 950. The dot marks the original brand value. Click any step to copy its hex.

MagentaPrimary · brand identity, links, key iconsbase 500 · #FF1E9A
MintAction · primary buttons & CTAs onlybase 500 · #11EE92
IndigoSecondary · trust bands, nav, headings on lightbase 700 · #1D2F6F
AquaTertiary · illustrations, info chips, chartsbase 300 · #6DD6DA
Accent · Citroneyebrows, highlights, badges — mode-paireddark #FEFFBE · light #6E651A
  • One accent, two legible expressions. Night Glow #FEFFBE (step 100) is the dark-mode accent only — on Paper it drops to ~1:1 and disappears. Its light-mode twin is Deep Citron #6E651A (step 700), the same yellow family deepened until it reads on white.
  • Accent ≠ Warning. The citron is for delight — "most popular", "new", eyebrow labels. Amber #F0A12E is for caution. They share a family but never a job, so a highlight never looks like an alarm.
Semantic states

The four product signals

Success stays your green. Warning and Error are new — chosen to harmonize with the warm palette and to read clearly apart from magenta. Each carries a fill (solid), a surface (tinted background), and a text/icon value tuned for the dark canvas.

Success
Mastery, completed, confirmed
fill#1FA34D
text on dark#54C178
surface#11271A
✓ Payment confirmed
Warning
Caution, limits, review needed
fill#F0A12E
text on dark#F4B752
surface#2A2110
! Approaching plan limit
Error
Destructive, failed, blocked
fill#F0433A
text on dark#F2756D
surface#2B1311
✕ Card was declined
Info
Tips, neutral notices
fill#2BAAB0
text on dark#6DD6DA
surface#10262A
i New lesson formats available
  • Error is not magenta. #F0433A (red, hue ~3°) sits far from #FF1E9A (magenta, hue ~327°), so "delete" never reads as "brand."
  • Warning is not Night Glow. Your #FEFFBE stays a decorative dark-mode accent; warnings use the real amber #F0A12E so caution actually registers.
  • Meaning never rides on color alone. Pair every state with an icon (✓ ! ✕ i) so it survives colorblindness — same rule your brand doc set for green.
Neutrals

Warm gray ramp

Built from your Paper → Stone → Ink → Charcoal temperature, extended to the 12 steps a product needs for borders, dividers, disabled states, and three levels of text.

Neutralsurfaces, borders, text · warm-toned, never pure gray
TokenValueDark modeLight mode
text / primary50 / 900Paper #F4F1EAInk #181817
text / secondary400 / 600#A9A296#6E6A60
text / muted500#7E796EStone #8E887B
border / divider700 / 200Paper @ 12%Sand #D9D4C7
disabled fill800 / 200#2E2C28#D9D4C7 @ 60%
Elevation

Surface layers

A product stacks panels, cards, modals and menus. Each mode gets four surface levels so depth stays legible without heavy shadows.

Dark mode · default
Canvas#22221F
Card / panel#181817
Raised / KPI tile#26261F
Overlay / modal / menu#2E2D27
Light mode
Canvas#F4F1EA
Card / panel#FFFFFF
Raised / KPI tile#FBFAF6
Overlay / modal / menu#FFFFFF +shadow

Buttons, badges, alerts and a dashboard slice — every value pulled from the tokens above.

Mint = primary action · Magenta = brand action · Ghost = secondary · Red = destructive

Charts

Data-visualization series

A categorical set ordered for maximum separation on the dark canvas. Aqua leads (it's the teaching color), magenta second, and the rest follow. Use in this order so adjacent series never blur.

Series 1 · Aqua
#6DD6DA
Series 2 · Magenta
#FF1E9A
Series 3 · Amber
#F4B752
Series 4 · Indigo 300
#6E84D1
Series 5 · Mint
#11EE92
Series 6 · Red 300
#F2756D
  • Sequential (one metric, low→high): use the Aqua ramp #E7F8F9 → #155457.
  • Gridlines & axes: Neutral 700 on dark, Neutral 200 on light — never a hue.
  • Mint and Success green stay out of charts unless the data literally means "action" or "complete," so the dashboard never cries wolf.
Handoff

Tokens as code

Drop-in CSS custom properties for the full product palette. The brand tokens match your v3 sheet exactly; everything else is the product layer.

/* Tungo — SaaS Product Palette · built on Brand System v3 */
:root{
  /* ── brand (unchanged) ── */
  --magenta-500:#FF1E9A; --magenta-600:#D6157F; --magenta-300:#FF74BC; --magenta-50:#FFE9F5;
  --mint-500:#11EE92;    --mint-600:#0CC97A;    --mint-300:#43E9A2;    --mint-50:#E3FCF1;
  --indigo-700:#1D2F6F;  --indigo-300:#6E84D1;  --indigo-50:#E8ECF7;
  --aqua-300:#6DD6DA;    --aqua-500:#2BAAB0;    --aqua-50:#E7F8F9;
  --accent-dark:#FEFFBE; --accent-light:#6E651A; /* Night Glow (dark) / Deep Citron (light) — mode-paired */

  /* ── semantic (new) ── */
  --success:#1FA34D; --success-text:#54C178; --success-surface:#11271A;
  --warning:#F0A12E; --warning-text:#F4B752; --warning-surface:#2A2110;
  --error:#F0433A;   --error-text:#F2756D;   --error-surface:#2B1311;
  --info:#2BAAB0;    --info-text:#6DD6DA;    --info-surface:#10262A;

  /* ── neutrals (warm) ── */
  --n-0:#FFFFFF;  --n-50:#F4F1EA; --n-100:#E9E5DB; --n-200:#D9D4C7;
  --n-300:#C2BCAD;--n-400:#A9A296;--n-500:#8E887B; --n-600:#6E6A60;
  --n-700:#4A463F;--n-800:#2E2C28;--n-850:#22221F; --n-900:#181817;

  /* ── surfaces · dark (default) ── */
  --bg-canvas:#22221F; --bg-card:#181817; --bg-raised:#26261F; --bg-overlay:#2E2D27;
  --text:#F4F1EA; --text-2:#A9A296; --text-3:#7E796E;
  --border:rgba(244,241,234,0.12);

  /* ── component contracts ── */
  --cta-bg:var(--mint-500);   --cta-text:var(--n-900);   /* buttons only, never white text */
  --brand-bg:var(--magenta-500); --brand-text:#FFFFFF;
  --danger-bg:var(--error);   --danger-text:#FFFFFF;
  --accent:var(--accent-dark); /* swaps to --accent-light in light mode */
  --focus-ring:var(--magenta-500);
}
/* light mode override */
[data-theme="light"]{
  --bg-canvas:#F4F1EA; --bg-card:#FFFFFF; --bg-raised:#FBFAF6; --bg-overlay:#FFFFFF;
  --text:#181817; --text-2:#6E6A60; --text-3:#8E887B; --border:#D9D4C7;
  --warning-text:#B66A12; --error-text:#D52A21; --info-text:#1F8A8F; --success-text:#198A40;
  --accent:var(--accent-light);
}

Typography

One typeface — Inter — across product and marketing. An 11-step scale, five weights, and a role for every style.

Inter
Ag
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ
abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz
0123456789 !@#$%^&*()

Font weight: 400

Font weight: 500
Aa

Font weight: 600

Bold
Font weight: 700
Type scale

The scale

Eleven sizes, each shown in all four weights with exact px / rem and letter-spacing. Sizes are desktop; columns wrap on narrow screens.

Semantic styles

Roles in the product

The scale mapped to the jobs it does in a SaaS UI. Use these names in design and code so "page title" always means one exact style — never a guess.

RoleStyleSize / LHWeightNotes
Marketing heroDisplay xl / 2xl60–72 / 72–90Extrabold 800Landing only. Tracking -3%.
Page titleDisplay sm30 / 38Semibold 600Top of an app screen.
Section headingDisplay xs24 / 32Semibold 600Groups within a page.
Card / panel titleText xl20 / 30Semibold 600Inside cards, modals.
SubheadingText lg18 / 28Semibold 600Minor titles, list groups.
Body (default)Text md16 / 24Regular 400The base. Inputs, copy.
Secondary / helpText sm14 / 20Regular 400Table cells, hints.
Button labelText sm / md14–16Semibold 600Same name through the flow.
Field labelText sm14 / 20Medium 500Sentence case.
Caption / metaText xs12 / 18Regular 400Timestamps, legal.
Eyebrow / labelText xs12 / 18Bold 700Uppercase, +14% tracking, Magenta.
Code / tokenMono sm13 / 20Regular 400JetBrains Mono.
In context

The roles, assembled

A single screen using nothing but the styles above — hierarchy built from size and weight, color used only to mark the eyebrow and the data.

Spanish · Level A2

Identifier usr_8f2a·track_es · synced via cloud
Best practices

Rules that keep it clean

  • Hierarchy by size + weight, not color. Magenta marks links, eyebrows and brand moments — never whole headings. A title is a title because it's bigger and heavier.
  • One step at a time. Don't jump Display sm → Text sm in adjacent elements; the scale's neighbouring steps are tuned to sit together.
  • Body never below 14px. Text xs (12px) is for captions and labels only — paragraphs live at 16px (Text md) for accessible reading.
  • Tabular numerals for data. Enable font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums in tables, KPIs and timers so digits don't shift width.
  • Sentence case everywhere except the uppercase eyebrow. "Start free trial", not "Start Free Trial".
  • Measure caps reading width. Keep long-form copy to ~60–75 characters per line; let Text lg (18px) carry lesson content.
  • Display tracking tightens; body stays at 0. -2% to -3% on Display sizes, neutral tracking on everything Text-sized.
Handoff

Tokens as code

The exact scale as CSS custom properties plus a Tailwind v4 @theme block — same --text-* naming so it drops straight into a project.

/* Tungo Typography — Inter */
:root{
  --font-sans:'Inter',system-ui,-apple-system,'Segoe UI',sans-serif;
  --font-mono:'JetBrains Mono',ui-monospace,Menlo,monospace;

  /* weights */
  --fw-regular:400; --fw-medium:500; --fw-semibold:600; --fw-bold:700;

  /* display  (font-size / line-height / letter-spacing) */
  --text-display-2xl:4.5rem;   --text-display-2xl--lh:5.625rem; --text-display-2xl--ls:-0.02em;  /* 72 / 90 */
  --text-display-xl:3.75rem;   --text-display-xl--lh:4.5rem;    --text-display-xl--ls:-0.02em;   /* 60 / 72 */
  --text-display-lg:3rem;      --text-display-lg--lh:3.75rem;   --text-display-lg--ls:-0.02em;   /* 48 / 60 */
  --text-display-md:2.25rem;   --text-display-md--lh:2.75rem;   --text-display-md--ls:-0.02em;   /* 36 / 44 */
  --text-display-sm:1.875rem;  --text-display-sm--lh:2.375rem;  /* 30 / 38 */
  --text-display-xs:1.5rem;    --text-display-xs--lh:2rem;      /* 24 / 32 */

  /* text */
  --text-xl:1.25rem;  --text-xl--lh:1.875rem;  /* 20 / 30 */
  --text-lg:1.125rem; --text-lg--lh:1.75rem;   /* 18 / 28 */
  --text-md:1rem;     --text-md--lh:1.5rem;    /* 16 / 24  · body */
  --text-sm:0.875rem; --text-sm--lh:1.25rem;   /* 14 / 20 */
  --text-xs:0.75rem;  --text-xs--lh:1.125rem;  /* 12 / 18 */
}
/* Tailwind v4 — globals.css */
@theme{
  --font-sans:'Inter',system-ui,sans-serif;
  --text-display-2xl:4.5rem;  --text-display-2xl--line-height:5.625rem;
  --text-display-xl:3.75rem;  --text-display-xl--line-height:4.5rem;
  --text-display-lg:3rem;     --text-display-lg--line-height:3.75rem;
  --text-display-md:2.25rem;  --text-display-md--line-height:2.75rem;
  --text-display-sm:1.875rem; --text-display-sm--line-height:2.375rem;
  --text-display-xs:1.5rem;   --text-display-xs--line-height:2rem;
  --text-xl:1.25rem;  --text-xl--line-height:1.875rem;
  --text-lg:1.125rem; --text-lg--line-height:1.75rem;
  --text-md:1rem;     --text-md--line-height:1.5rem;
  --text-sm:0.875rem; --text-sm--line-height:1.25rem;
  --text-xs:0.75rem;  --text-xs--line-height:1.125rem;
}

Icons

The full Untitled UI line set — {ALL_N} icons on a 24px grid, 2px stroke, drawn with currentColor so each inherits the theme color. Click any icon to copy its SVG.

All icons 1173
No icons match that search.
In React

Install & use

In your product, the official package ships these as React components that already follow the currentColor and size conventions.

npm i @untitledui/icons
// import individual icons by name
import ArrowDown from "@untitledui/icons/ArrowDown";

<ArrowDown />
<ArrowDown size={16} />
<ArrowDown className="text-magenta size-5" />
Guidelines

Using icons

  • Style. 24×24 box, 2px stroke, round caps and joins, fill:none — optically balanced with Inter at body sizes.
  • Color. Every icon uses stroke="currentColor", so it follows the parent's color: Magenta for brand, Stone for muted, the semantic colors for status.
  • Sizing. 16px inline with text, 20–24px in buttons and nav, 32px+ for empty states. Scale the box, keep the stroke at 2px.
  • Licensing. Untitled UI Icons are free in unlimited personal and commercial projects. This page embeds your own licensed copy for your design system; their license doesn't allow re-selling or redistributing the set as its own product. See untitledui.com/license.

Spacing, radius & grids

A single 4px soft-grid system for spacing, sizing, radius, widths, and layout. Working from a fixed set of values removes guesswork and gives every screen the same vertical rhythm. Click any value to copy.

Why

Why a spacing system

Like the colour scale, a defined spacing system lets you work faster and more consistently — you design with a limited, memorable set of options instead of arbitrary numbers. That consistency is what creates a familiar rhythm and order across the product, and it's what lets developers rebuild a design accurately.

4px soft grid — every value divisible by 4, still aligning to an 8px grid
  • Start from a 4px soft grid. Every spacing value is divisible by 4 across components and layouts — looser than a rigid 8px hard grid, but still aligned to it.
  • Then limit the options. Rather than choosing between 124px and 128px, pick the one predefined value (128px) and reuse it everywhere.
  • The scale runs 0 → 1,920px, covering everything from a 2px nudge to a full large-screen layout.
Spacing · variables

Spacing scale

The named spacing tokens — derived from the primitive scale — for gaps, padding, and margins across every component and layout.

NamerempxScale
spacing-none0rem0px
spacing-xxs0.125rem2px
spacing-xs0.25rem4px
spacing-sm0.375rem6px
spacing-md0.5rem8px
spacing-lg0.75rem12px
spacing-xl1rem16px
spacing-2xl1.25rem20px
spacing-3xl1.5rem24px
spacing-4xl2rem32px
spacing-5xl2.5rem40px
spacing-6xl3rem48px
spacing-7xl4rem64px
spacing-8xl5rem80px
spacing-9xl6rem96px
spacing-10xl8rem128px
spacing-11xl10rem160px
Radius

Corner radius

Border-radius tokens for rounding images, buttons, cards, and any other element — the same predefined approach as spacing, derived from the primitive scale.

radius-none
radius-xxs
radius-xs
radius-sm
radius-md
radius-lg
radius-xl
radius-2xl
radius-3xl
radius-4xl
radius-full
NamerempxRadius
radius-none0rem0px
radius-xxs0.125rem2px
radius-xs0.25rem4px
radius-sm0.375rem6px
radius-md0.5rem8px
radius-lg0.625rem10px
radius-xl0.75rem12px
radius-2xl1rem16px
radius-3xl1.25rem20px
radius-4xl1.5rem24px
radius-full∞9999px
Widths · variables

Width scale

Predefined max-widths for wrapping content — sections, text columns, and page headings — so layouts cap at consistent, readable sizes.

NamerempxScale
width-xxs20rem320px
width-xs24rem384px
width-sm30rem480px
width-md35rem560px
width-lg40rem640px
width-xl48rem768px
width-2xl64rem1,024px
width-3xl80rem1,280px
width-4xl90rem1,440px
width-5xl100rem1,600px
width-6xl120rem1,920px
Containers · variables

Container padding & width

Dedicated variables for container padding and max-width, kept separate from spacing and width tokens so you can re-flow the whole layout from one place.

NamerempxScale
container-padding-mobile1rem16px
container-padding-desktop2rem32px
container-max-width-desktop80rem1,280px
Paragraph · variables

Paragraph max-width

Long-form text gets its own max-width so line length stays readable. Aim for 45–85 characters per line; beyond that, the eye has to travel too far and reading gets tiring. 720px is the default.

NamerempxScale
paragraph-max-width45rem720px

Tungo teaches a language through short daily lessons that build on each other — a few minutes of focused practice, spaced out, beats a long cram session. Each unit pairs new vocabulary with the grammar that makes it usable, then reinforces both with listening and recall before moving on.

max-width 720px · ~75 characters per line
Grids

Layout grids

Layout grids define structure, hierarchy, and rhythm. Three responsive breakpoints carry the columns, gutters, and side margins; content caps at the inner width.

Desktop1,280px · 12 columns · 32px gutters · 32px margins
content area 1,216px
Tablet768px · 6 columns · 32px gutters · 32px margins
content area 704px
Mobile375px · 4 columns · 16px gutters · 16px margins
content area 343px
Container column presets

Inside a container, lay content out on one of these automatic column counts.

12 columns
6 columns
5 columns
3 columns
2 columns
Primitives

Spacing primitives

The underlying numeric scale every token is derived from: one unit = 0.25rem / 4px, so 16 is twice the spacing of 8. Edit these to reshape the whole system.

TokenrempxScale
00rem0px
0.50.125rem2px
10.25rem4px
1.50.375rem6px
20.5rem8px
30.75rem12px
41rem16px
51.25rem20px
61.5rem24px
82rem32px
102.5rem40px
123rem48px
164rem64px
205rem80px
246rem96px
328rem128px
4010rem160px
4812rem192px
5614rem224px
6416rem256px
8020rem320px
9624rem384px
12030rem480px
14035rem560px
16040rem640px
18045rem720px
19248rem768px
25664rem1,024px
32080rem1,280px
36090rem1,440px
400100rem1,600px
480120rem1,920px
Off-grid

When 4px doesn't fit

Not everything lands on a 4px grid, and that's fine. The button is the classic example.

  • Button lg is 44px so it sits between Button md (40px) and Button xl (48px). To hit 44px with a 20px icon, it needs 10px top/bottom padding — and 10px isn't on a 4px grid.
  • Icon buttons trim 2px of left padding (16px → 14px). Icons are framed with ~2px of optical buffer, so removing 2px keeps the button visually centred.
  • The heights still align (40 / 44 / 48px all on the grid) and the buttons look balanced — don't stress the occasional off-grid value.
Best practices

Rules of thumb

  • Default to multiples of 8px for measurements, spacing, and positioning — picture an 8×8 grid.
  • Drop to 4px for fine adjustments when 8px is too coarse.
  • Line things up — vertically and horizontally — wherever possible.
  • Commit to the set. Use the defined spacing tokens across every component and layout instead of one-off values.
Export

Tokens

/* Tungo spacing, radius, width & container tokens */
:root{
  /* spacing */
  --spacing-none:0; --spacing-xxs:.125rem; --spacing-xs:.25rem; --spacing-sm:.375rem;
  --spacing-md:.5rem; --spacing-lg:.75rem; --spacing-xl:1rem; --spacing-2xl:1.25rem;
  --spacing-3xl:1.5rem; --spacing-4xl:2rem; --spacing-5xl:2.5rem; --spacing-6xl:3rem;
  --spacing-7xl:4rem; --spacing-8xl:5rem; --spacing-9xl:6rem; --spacing-10xl:8rem; --spacing-11xl:10rem;
  /* radius */
  --radius-none:0; --radius-xxs:.125rem; --radius-xs:.25rem; --radius-sm:.375rem; --radius-md:.5rem;
  --radius-lg:.625rem; --radius-xl:.75rem; --radius-2xl:1rem; --radius-3xl:1.25rem; --radius-4xl:1.5rem; --radius-full:9999px;
  /* widths */
  --width-xxs:20rem; --width-xs:24rem; --width-sm:30rem; --width-md:35rem; --width-lg:40rem;
  --width-xl:48rem; --width-2xl:64rem; --width-3xl:80rem; --width-4xl:90rem; --width-5xl:100rem; --width-6xl:120rem;
  /* containers + paragraph */
  --container-padding-mobile:1rem; --container-padding-desktop:2rem; --container-max-width-desktop:80rem;
  --paragraph-max-width:45rem;
}

Effect styles

Depth and realism on the z-axis — a shadow scale for elevation, backdrop blurs for glass surfaces, and focus rings for accessible keyboard navigation.

Shadows

Elevation scale

Seven levels of shadow position elements on a z-axis — the higher the level, the more the element lifts off the page. Click a card to copy its box-shadow.

shadow-xs
shadow-sm
shadow-md
shadow-lg
shadow-xl
shadow-2xl
shadow-3xl
  • Match elevation to importance. Cards and inputs sit low (xs–sm); popovers and dropdowns mid (md–lg); modals and dialogs high (xl–2xl); only the topmost layers use 3xl.
  • One light source. Every shadow falls straight down with the same soft, neutral tint, so the whole UI feels lit from one direction.
  • Shadows reference a near-black base at low opacity (2–18%). Keep them subtle — depth should be felt, not seen.
Backdrop blur

Glass surfaces

Backdrop blurs frost whatever sits behind a surface — used for sticky bars, overlays, and modals. Four strengths, each shown on a light and a dark panel over the same backdrop.

backdrop-blur-smlight
backdrop-blur-smdark
backdrop-blur-mdlight
backdrop-blur-mddark
backdrop-blur-lglight
backdrop-blur-lgdark
backdrop-blur-xllight
backdrop-blur-xldark
Focus rings

Single layer

For elements that don't carry a shadow — toggles, checkboxes, radios. A solid inner line plus a soft outer halo, in brand and error.

focus-ring
brand-500
focus-ring-error
error-500
Focus rings

With shadows

Elements that already cast a shadow — like buttons — combine the ring with an elevation shadow so both read at once.

focus-ring-shadow-xs
brand-500shadow-xs
focus-ring-shadow-sm
brand-500shadow-sm
focus-ring-error-shadow-xs
error-500shadow-xs
Focus rings

With shadows + skeuomorphic

The richest variant layers the ring, an elevation shadow, and subtle inner shadows for a tactile, slightly raised feel.

focus-ring-shadow-xs-skeumorphic
brand-500shadow-xssubtle inner shadows
focus-ring-error-shadow-xs-skeumorphic
error-500shadow-xssubtle inner shadows
Skeuomorphism

Subtle depth

Buttons and featured icons carry a gentle skeuomorphic treatment — a whisper of depth from a couple of shadow effects. It's a deliberate, accessible nod to physicality; drop it for a flat look anytime.

Flat Skeuomorphic
  • It's just shadows. The effect is a fill plus a drop shadow and a pair of subtle inner shadows — nothing heavy.
  • Easy to remove. Because depth lives in effect styles, switching to a flat style is a one-line change at the component level.
Accessibility

Focus styles matter

  • Focus rings stand in for the mouse pointer. They show keyboard and assistive-tech users exactly where they are — never remove them without an equally clear replacement.
  • Error focus uses red, so an invalid field reads as both focused and wrong.
  • Rings ride on a colour token. Brand and error rings reference the same brand-500 / error-500 values as the rest of the system, so a palette change updates them automatically.
Export

Tokens

/* Tungo effect styles */
:root{
  /* shadows (elevation) */
  --shadow-xs:0 1px 2px rgba(10,13,18,.05);
  --shadow-sm:0 1px 3px rgba(10,13,18,.10),0 1px 2px -1px rgba(10,13,18,.10);
  --shadow-md:0 4px 8px -2px rgba(10,13,18,.10),0 2px 4px -2px rgba(10,13,18,.06);
  --shadow-lg:0 12px 16px -4px rgba(10,13,18,.08),0 4px 6px -2px rgba(10,13,18,.03);
  --shadow-xl:0 20px 24px -4px rgba(10,13,18,.08),0 8px 8px -4px rgba(10,13,18,.03);
  --shadow-2xl:0 24px 48px -12px rgba(10,13,18,.18);
  --shadow-3xl:0 32px 64px -12px rgba(10,13,18,.14);
  /* backdrop blur */
  --blur-sm:4px; --blur-md:8px; --blur-lg:16px; --blur-xl:24px;
  /* focus rings */
  --focus-ring:0 0 0 1.5px #FF1E9A,0 0 0 4px rgba(255,30,154,.22);
  --focus-ring-error:0 0 0 1.5px #F0433A,0 0 0 4px rgba(240,67,58,.22);
  --focus-ring-shadow-xs:var(--focus-ring),0 1px 2px rgba(10,13,18,.06);
}

Buttons

Buttons communicate the actions a user can take. Built from the colour tokens, arranged in a clear hierarchy, and optically balanced whether or not they carry an icon.

Hierarchy

Four levels

Every screen has one primary action and a descending order of less important ones. The button's weight signals its importance: Primary (filled) → Secondary (outline) → Tertiary (ghost) → Link (text).

  • One primary per view. Mint is the brightest thing on the page — two primaries cancel each other out.
  • Labels are verbs. "Start free trial", not "Submit", and the label keeps the same name through the whole flow.
  • Without hierarchy there's no cue — users have to stop and read every action. Differentiate by weight so the main path is obvious.
Color

Colour families

The same four levels in each family. Neutral for everyday actions (mint primary), Brand for product moments (magenta), Destructive for consequential actions (red).

Neutral
Brand
Destructive
Size

Five sizes

From sm (36px) to 2xl (56px). Heights stay on the 4px grid; pick by surface density.

States

Interaction states

Default, hover (lighten/raise), focus (a 3px ring for keyboard users), disabled, and loading with an inline spinner.

Hover any button above to see the live hover transition.

With icons

Leading, trailing & icon-only

Icons use currentColor so they match the label. Button text carries 2px of extra side padding so an icon never throws the button off balance.

Destructive

Destructive actions

Not every negative action needs a big red button — a secondary usually does the job. Reserve the filled red primary for when the destructive action is the main, highly-consequential one.

Social

Social buttons

For sign-in and sign-up. Three styles — filled, outline, and icon-only — in full-width and compact forms.

The square marks are logo slots. Drop in each provider's official logo and brand colour following their own brand guidelines — Tungo doesn't ship third-party logos or the App Store / Google Play badges, which are trademarked and must come from each vendor.
Guidelines

Best practices

  • Buttons should look like buttons. A rounded rectangle reads as actionable instantly; the more it strays, the harder users have to hunt for the action.
  • Respect hierarchy. One obvious primary, then secondary and tertiary in descending weight.
  • Mint always carries Ink text (#181817), never white — on light surfaces add a 1px Ink-20% border for edge definition.
  • Danger is red, never magenta — a destructive action should never look like a brand moment.
  • Optical balance. Text is padded 2px on each side and the button's own padding reduced by 2px, so icon and text buttons look evenly centred.
  • Subtle depth. A whisper-soft shadow gives buttons a tactile, slightly skeuomorphic feel; drop it for a flat look if you prefer.
In React

Props

// variant: primary | secondary | tertiary | link
// color: neutral | brand | destructive   size: sm | md | lg | xl | 2xl
<Button color="brand" size="md">Upgrade plan</Button>
<Button variant="secondary" iconLeading={Plus}>Add team</Button>
<Button color="destructive" iconLeading={Trash}>Delete workspace</Button>
<Button isLoading>Submitting…</Button>
<Button iconOnly icon={Plus} aria-label="Add" />

Button groups

A set of related actions joined into one segmented control — shared borders, rounded only on the ends. Good for toolbars, filters, and view switches.

Basic

Segmented actions

Equal-weight actions sit side by side with hairline dividers. The whole group reads as one control.

With icons

Leading icon

Each segment can lead with an icon — drawn with currentColor, so they follow the control's text colour.

With dot

Status dot

A leading dot turns segments into status switches. The third here is disabled.

Disabled

Whole group

Disable the entire control when none of its actions are currently available.

Disabled item

Individual item

Or disable a single segment while the rest stay active — here, Delete.

Selection

Single select

Used as a toggle, one segment stays selected — ideal for a date range or view filter. Click to switch.

Multiple selection

Multi select

Allow more than one active segment at a time. Click each to toggle it on or off.

Guidelines

Using button groups

  • Keep it to peers. Group only actions of equal weight — never mix a primary CTA into a segmented group.
  • Two to five segments. Past five, reach for a dropdown or tabs instead.
  • Selection vs actions. Use the selected state for toggles (filters, ranges); for one-shot actions (Archive, Edit, Delete) leave them unselected.
  • Short labels. One or two words per segment keeps the control compact and scannable.
In React

Props

// selectionMode: none | single | multiple
<ButtonGroup>
  <ButtonGroupItem iconLeading={Archive}>Archive</ButtonGroupItem>
  <ButtonGroupItem iconLeading={Edit}>Edit</ButtonGroupItem>
  <ButtonGroupItem iconLeading={Trash} isDisabled>Delete</ButtonGroupItem>
</ButtonGroup>

<ButtonGroup selectionMode="single" defaultSelectedKeys={["today"]}>
  <ButtonGroupItem id="today">Today</ButtonGroupItem>
  <ButtonGroupItem id="tomorrow">Tomorrow</ButtonGroupItem>
  <ButtonGroupItem id="week">This week</ButtonGroupItem>
</ButtonGroup>

Badges

Compact labels for status, categories, counts, and metadata. Three styles — pill, rounded, and modern — in three sizes, coloured straight from the Tungo palette.

Styles

Three shapes

Pill is the default — fully rounded, best for status and tags. Rounded squares off slightly for denser, table-like UI. Modern sits on a raised surface with a hairline and soft shadow, pairing with a coloured status dot.

PillRoundedModern
type="pill-color" · "badge-color" · "badge-modern"
Color

The full set

Nine colours, each a tint of a palette hue with a matching outline and text. Reach for Gray by default; let the semantic colours (Success, Warning, Error) carry status, and the brand hues carry category.

GrayBrandIndigoAquaMintSuccessWarningErrorAccent
GrayBrandIndigoAquaMintSuccessWarningErrorAccent
  • Gray is the resting state. Use it for neutral metadata — counts, versions, plan names — so coloured badges stay meaningful.
  • Semantic colours mean status, not decoration: Success for active/paid, Warning for trials ending, Error for failed/overdue.
  • Magenta is brand, reserved for product moments (New, Pro, a featured tag) — never for plain labels.
Modern

Modern with status dot

The modern style keeps a neutral surface and lets a coloured dot do the signalling — ideal in tables and lists where many badges sit together.

GrayBrandIndigoAquaMintSuccessWarning
Size

Three sizes

sm for dense tables, md as the default, lg for headers and empty states.

SmallMediumLarge
With dot

Status dots

A leading dot turns a badge into a status pill. The dot inherits the badge colour, so meaning and hue stay in sync.

OnlineAwayOfflineErrorLive
With icon

Leading & trailing icons

Any of the 1173 icons drops straight in — they're drawn with currentColor, so they take the badge's text colour automatically.

GrayBrandIndigoAquaMintSuccess
UpgradeStreakA1 levelBetaAnnouncement
Dismissible

With close

A trailing close button makes a badge removable — for filters, selected tags, and tokenised inputs. Click an × to dismiss it.

GrayBrandIndigoAquaMintSuccess
Icon only

Icon badges

Drop the label for a compact square. Keep these to universally understood icons, and add a tooltip or aria-label for meaning.

In React

Props

Mirrors the Untitled UI API: a color, a type, a size, plus dot / icon / image / close variants.

// color: gray | brand | indigo | aqua | mint | success | warning | error | accent
// type:  pill-color | badge-color | badge-modern   size: sm | md | lg
<Badge color="success" type="pill-color" size="md">Active</Badge>
<BadgeWithDot color="warning">Trial ending</BadgeWithDot>
<BadgeWithIcon color="brand" iconLeading={Zap}>7-day streak</BadgeWithIcon>
<BadgeWithButton color="gray" onButtonClick={remove}>Spanish</BadgeWithButton>
<BadgeIcon color="brand" icon={Star} />

Badge groups

A badge paired with a line of supporting text in one container — the announcement pattern. Two styles (pill and modern), two layouts (badge leading or trailing), in the five status colours.

Example

Anatomy

A solid inner badge carries the label; the supporting text explains it. Optionally a trailing arrow turns the whole group into a clickable announcement.

New featureWe've just released a new feature
Pill · leading

Pill, badge leading

The container is a soft tinted pill; the badge sits on the left. The default style for inline announcements.

Default
Version 4.0We've just released a new featureNew featureWe've just released a new featureErrorThere was a problem with that actionWarningJust to let you know this might be a problemSuccessYou've updated your profile and details
With status dot
New featureWe've just released a new featureNew featureWe've just released a new featureErrorThere was a problem with that actionWarningJust to let you know this might be a problemSuccessYou've updated your profile and details
Pill · trailing

Pill, badge trailing

Same pill container with the badge moved to the end — useful when the message should read first. Note the error badge becomes a Fix now call to action.

Default
We've just released a new featureVersion 4.0We've just released a new featureNew featureThere was a problem with that actionFix nowJust to let you know this might be a problemWarningYou've updated your profile and detailsSuccess
With status dot
We've just released a new featureNew featureWe've just released a new featureNew featureThere was a problem with that actionFix nowJust to let you know this might be a problemWarningYou've updated your profile and detailsSuccess
Modern · leading

Modern, badge leading

A neutral surface with a hairline and soft shadow, pairing with a bordered badge — quieter, good on busy pages.

Default
Version 4.0We've just released a new featureNew featureWe've just released a new featureErrorThere was a problem with that actionWarningJust to let you know this might be a problemSuccessYou've updated your profile and details
With status dot
New featureWe've just released a new featureNew featureWe've just released a new featureErrorThere was a problem with that actionWarningJust to let you know this might be a problemSuccessYou've updated your profile and details
Modern · trailing

Modern, badge trailing

The modern container with the badge at the end.

Default
We've just released a new featureVersion 4.0We've just released a new featureNew featureThere was a problem with that actionFix nowJust to let you know this might be a problemWarningYou've updated your profile and detailsSuccess
With status dot
We've just released a new featureNew featureWe've just released a new featureNew featureThere was a problem with that actionFix nowJust to let you know this might be a problemWarningYou've updated your profile and detailsSuccess
Guidelines

Using badge groups

  • One per surface. A badge group is a spotlight — a release note, a status callout, a banner. More than one and they stop standing out.
  • Badge is the category, text is the detail. Keep the badge to a word or two (New, Error, Success) and let the supporting text carry the message.
  • Colour follows meaning — the same semantic rules as badges: Success for done, Warning for heads-up, Error for problems, Brand for product news, Gray for neutral.
  • Add the trailing arrow only when it links somewhere. If the whole group is clickable, the arrow signals it; otherwise leave it off.
In React

Props

// color: gray | brand | error | warning | success
// type:  pill-color | modern   align: leading | trailing   dot: boolean
<BadgeGroup color="gray" type="pill-color" align="leading" addonText="Version 4.0">
  We've just released a new feature
</BadgeGroup>

<BadgeGroup color="error" type="modern" align="trailing" dot addonText="Fix now">
  There was a problem with that action
</BadgeGroup>

Checkboxes & radios

Selection controls for forms and lists. Checkboxes toggle independent options (and can be indeterminate); radios pick one from a set. Both come in two sizes with full default, hover, focus, and disabled states.

Checkbox

Values

Three values: unchecked, checked, and indeterminate — the dash signals a parent whose children are partly selected.

Checkbox · states

States & sizes

Every value across default, hover, focus (a 4px brand ring), and disabled — in medium (20px) and small (16px).

Medium · 20px
UncheckedCheckedIndeterminate
Default
Hover
Focus
Disabled
Small · 16px
UncheckedCheckedIndeterminate
Default
Hover
Focus
Disabled
Checkbox

With label

Pair the control with a title and optional hint. The whole row is the hit area — click any to toggle.

Radio

Values

Two values: unselected and selected. Radios are circular to read as mutually exclusive.

Radio · states

States & sizes

The same default / hover / focus / disabled states, in medium and small.

Medium · 20px
UnselectedSelected
Default
Hover
Focus
Disabled
Small · 16px
UnselectedSelected
Default
Hover
Focus
Disabled
Radio

With label

In a group only one stays selected — click any row to switch.

Guidelines

Checkbox vs radio

  • Checkbox for independent toggles — any number can be on, including none. Use the indeterminate dash for a "select all" whose children are partly checked.
  • Radio for one-of-many — exactly one option is always selected. If a choice can be cleared, use a checkbox instead.
  • The label is part of the control. Clicking the title or hint toggles it, so the hit area is comfortably large.
  • Two or more, always visible. For a single on/off setting reach for a toggle; for more than ~6 mutually-exclusive options use a select.
  • Focus is never removed. The 4px ring keeps keyboard users oriented; error states reuse the same ring in red.
In React

Props

// size: sm | md
<Checkbox defaultSelected>Remember me</Checkbox>
<Checkbox isIndeterminate />
<Checkbox isDisabled>Remember me</Checkbox>

<RadioGroup defaultValue="remember">
  <Radio value="remember">Remember me</Radio>
  <Radio value="forget">Don't remember</Radio>
</RadioGroup>

Dropdowns

Dropdown menus group related actions inside a compact subview — reducing clutter when several actions share limited space. A trigger opens a floating menu of items, with optional icons, shortcuts, selection, submenus, search, and account headers.

Triggers

Six ways to open one

Any control can open a menu. Click a trigger below to open it; click away to dismiss.

Button
My profile
Account settings
Notifications
Sign out
Link
Can view
Can edit
Owner
Remove access
Icon
Edit
Duplicate
Archive
Delete
Avatar
OROlivia Rhyeolivia@tungo.app
View profile⌘K→P
Settings⌘S
Dark mode
Support
Switch account
OROlivia Rhye
SHSienna Hewitt
Add account
Sign out
© Tungov1.0.0
Account card
OROlivia Rhyeolivia@tungo.app
View profile⌘K→P
Settings⌘S
Dark mode
Support
Switch account
OROlivia Rhye
SHSienna Hewitt
Add account
Sign out
© Tungov1.0.0
Breadcrumb
OROlivia Rhyeolivia@tungo.app
View profile⌘K→P
Settings⌘S
Dark mode
Support
Switch account
OROlivia Rhye
SHSienna Hewitt
Add account
Sign out
© Tungov1.0.0
Content

Icons, shortcuts & submenus

Items can carry a leading icon, a keyboard shortcut, a destructive treatment, and a chevron that opens a nested submenu on hover.

Cut⌘X
Copy⌘C
Paste⌘V
Edit
Duplicate
Delete
View details
Open
Rename
Move to
Selection

Single select

A menu can act as a picker — one item stays selected, marked with a trailing check. Click to switch.

Can view
Can edit
Owner
Remove access
Icon menu

Checks, dots & nested tools

A richer menu: leading checks for toggles, a status dot beside an account, and a More tools submenu.

Back
Forward
Reload⌘R
Edit page
Add to favorites
Show bookmarks
Show full URLs
Olivia Rhye
Sienna Hewitt
More tools
Save as
Cut⌘X
Copy⌘C
Developer
With search

Searchable list

For long lists, add a search field and selectable rows with avatars — ideal for assigning people or managing access.

OROlivia Rhye
PBPhoenix Baker
LSLana Steiner
DWDemi Wilkinson
CWCandice Wu
NCNatali Craig
Integrations

Action list

A flat list of actions, grouped by dividers — copy/export formats and "open in" destinations.

Copy as
View as markdown
Copy as markdown
Open in v0
Open in Claude
Open in Cursor
Open in ChatGPT
Open in Figma
Create GitHub Gist
Account

Account menu

The fullest pattern — a header card with avatar, name, and email; grouped actions; a switch-account section; and a footer with the version. This is what the avatar, account-card, and breadcrumb triggers above open.

OROlivia Rhyeolivia@tungo.app
View profile⌘K→P
Settings⌘S
Dark mode
Support
Switch account
OROlivia Rhye
SHSienna Hewitt
Add account
Sign out
© Tungov1.0.0
Anatomy

Item states & parts

Default, hover, focus, and disabled rows, plus the optional parts: shortcut, trailing check, submenu chevron, status dot, and avatar.

Default
Hovered
Focused
Disabled
With shortcut⌘C
Selected
With submenu
Child one
Child two
Status item
ORPerson
Guidelines

Using dropdowns

  • Group by relatedness, separate by dividers. Keep destructive actions (Delete, Remove, Sign out) in their own group, usually last.
  • Shortcuts on the right, aligned. Show keyboard shortcuts for power actions; keep them muted so labels lead.
  • Submenus for overflow only. Nest a level when a group has its own sub-actions — don't go more than one level deep.
  • Search past ~7 items. Long people or option lists get a search field pinned to the top.
  • Anchor to the trigger and flip to stay on-screen near edges; close on outside-click, item-select, or Escape.
In React

Props

// React Aria menu primitives
<MenuTrigger>
  <Button>Actions</Button>
  <Menu>
    <MenuItem iconLeading={Scissors} shortcut="⌘X">Cut</MenuItem>
    <MenuItem iconLeading={Copy} shortcut="⌘C">Copy</MenuItem>
    <MenuSeparator />
    <MenuItem isDestructive iconLeading={Trash}>Delete</MenuItem>
    <SubmenuTrigger>
      <MenuItem>View details</MenuItem>
      <Menu>…</Menu>
    </SubmenuTrigger>
  </Menu>
</MenuTrigger>

Inputs

Input fields let users enter text in forms and dialogs. Each field is a label, a control with optional add-ons, and a hint or error line. On mobile, text is at least 16px to avoid auto-zoom.

Anatomy

Default

Label with required marker, the control, and a hint line. Focus shows a 4px brand ring; the help icon sits at the trailing edge.

This is a hint text to help user.
States

Every state

Default, hover, focus, filled, disabled, and error — the error adds a red border, an alert icon, and a red message in place of the hint.

This is an error message.
Size

Three sizes

sm (36px), md (40px), and lg (44px) heights.

This is a hint text to help user.
This is a hint text to help user.
This is a hint text to help user.
Add-ons

Icons, dropdowns, text & buttons

The control flexes to hold a leading icon, a leading or trailing dropdown, a static text prefix, a trailing action button, or a payment-card icon.

This is a hint text to help user.
This is a hint text to help user.
$
This is a hint text to help user.
https://
This is a hint text to help user.
This is a hint text to help user.
This is a hint text to help user.
Specialised

Password, date, file & number

Password toggles visibility; date shows a format mask; file upload pairs a name with a browse button; number fields step up and down (try them).

Must be at least 8 characters.
This is a hint text to help user.
No file chosen
SVG, PNG, JPG or GIF (max. 800x400px).
This is a hint text to help user.
This is a hint text to help user.
Verification code

One box per digit

Large, separate boxes that auto-advance as you type. For more than four digits, split into groups so they're easier to read and remember.

This is a hint text to help user.
This is a hint text to help user.
Tags

Tag input

Tokenised entry — type and press Enter to add, click the × to remove. Tags can sit inside the field or below it.

DesignEngineering
This is a hint text to help user.
DesignMarketing
This is a hint text to help user.
Textarea

Multi-line

For longer text. Same label, hint, and error treatment; resizes vertically.

This is a hint text to help user.
This is an error message.
Guidelines

Using inputs

  • Always label. A visible label above the field beats a placeholder — placeholders vanish on type and fail accessibility.
  • Hint vs error. Show a neutral hint by default; on validation failure swap it for a red message and mark the field with the error border and alert icon.
  • 16px on mobile. Keep input text at least 16px so mobile browsers don't auto-zoom on focus.
  • Group verification digits. Past four digits, split into sections to aid recall.
  • Add-ons clarify, not clutter. Use a prefix/suffix (https://, USD, $) instead of repeating it in the hint.
In React

Props

// size: sm | md | lg
<Input label="Email" hint="This is a hint text to help user." isRequired />
<Input label="Email" iconLeading={Mail} />
<Input label="Email" isInvalid errorMessage="This is an error message." />
<Input label="Password" type="password" />
<InputGroup label="Sale amount" addonLeading="$" addonTrailing="USD" />
<TextArea label="Description" rows={4} />
<PinInput length={4} />
<TagInput label="Tags" />

Featured icons

Free and open-source featured-icon components for modern apps and websites — built with React Aria and styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗
Get started

Installation

Add this featured-icon component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add featured-icon
// 1. copy FeaturedIcon.tsx into your components folder
// 2. import and use it
import { FeaturedIcon } from "@/components/featured-icon";
<FeaturedIcon icon={CheckCircle} theme="light" color="brand" />

Featured icon examples

Below are examples and variations of this featured-icon component.

Overview

One icon, six treatments

A single icon framed in a styled container — used to anchor empty states, feature cards, alerts, and onboarding steps. The same icon rendered in each theme: light, gradient, dark, outline, modern, and modern neue.

Theme

Light

A pale tinted circle with a strong-coloured icon and a soft outer halo — the quietest, most common treatment. Five colours across sm (32px), md (40px), lg (48px), and xl (56px).

Theme

Gradient

A saturated gradient-filled circle with a white icon — bolder, for primary moments.

Theme

Dark

A solid-filled rounded square with a white icon — the highest-contrast option.

Theme

Outline

A transparent circle ringed by concentric strokes in the colour — light and decorative.

Theme

Modern

A neutral rounded square on the surface colour with a hairline border and a soft shadow — understated and themeless.

Theme

Modern neue

Like modern, with a subtle skeuomorphic lift — an inner highlight and a slightly deeper drop shadow.

Shape

Circle or square

Shape is an independent axis — every theme works as a circle or a rounded square. The grids above use each theme's default shape; here's the same theme both ways.

light
circlesquare
gradient
circlesquare
dark
circlesquare
outline
circlesquare
modern
circlesquare
neue
circlesquare
Guidelines

Using featured icons

  • Match colour to meaning. Use success/warning/error for status moments and brand for neutral emphasis — don't colour decoratively.
  • One per block. A featured icon anchors a single message (empty state, alert, step); rows of them compete for attention.
  • Size to context. sm/md sit inline beside text; lg/xl headline an empty state or modal.
  • Light and outline for calm, gradient and dark for emphasis. Modern and modern neue stay neutral when colour would distract.
  • The inner icon keeps a 2px stroke at every size so it never looks thin or heavy.
In React

Props

// theme: light | gradient | dark | outline | modern | modern-neue
// color: brand | gray | error | warning | success   size: sm | md | lg | xl
<FeaturedIcon icon={CheckCircle} theme="light" color="brand" size="md" />
<FeaturedIcon icon={AlertTriangle} theme="gradient" color="warning" size="lg" />
<FeaturedIcon icon={Trash} theme="dark" color="error" size="xl" />

Progress indicators

Free and open-source progress indicators — linear bars, circles, and half-circle gauges — built with React Aria and styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗
Get started

Installation

Add the progress components with the CLI, or copy them in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add progress-indicators progress-circles
// linear bars
import { ProgressBar } from "@/components/base/progress-indicators";
// circles & half-circles
import { ProgressBarCircle, ProgressBarHalfCircle } from "@/components/base/progress-circles";

Progress examples

Below are examples and variations of these progress components.

Playground

Live value

All three share the same min / max / value contract — drag to drive a bar, a circle, and a gauge at once.

40%
Active users40%
Active users40%
Progress bars

Linear — label positions

Bars indicate the percentage completed of a task. The percentage label can sit in five positions, set via labelPosition.

Default
Text right
40%
Text bottom
40%
Top floating
40%
Bottom floating
40%
Progress bars

Across the scale

The fill grows from 0% to 100% with rounded ends on a light track.

0%
20%
40%
60%
80%
100%
Progress circles

Circle — five sizes

Rings useful for showing how much of a resource or plan remains, or to prompt completion (e.g. filling out a profile). Sizes xxs → lg.

40%
40%
40%
40%
40%
Progress circles

Circle — with label

A label joins the percentage — centred inside from xs up; for xxs the percentage sits in the ring with the label beneath.

40%
Users
Active users40%
Active users40%
Active users40%
Active users40%
Progress circles

Half circle — five sizes

A gauge variant — the same value across xxs → lg.

40%
40%
40%
40%
40%
Progress circles

Half circle — with label

Gauge with a label below the percentage.

40%
Users
Active users40%
Active users40%
Active users40%
Active users40%
Guidelines

Using progress

  • Bars for tasks, circles for resources. Linear bars suit step/upload progress; rings and gauges suit "how much is left" and dashboard stats.
  • Always show the value when the number matters (uploads, limits); hide it when the bar is purely ambient.
  • Rounded ends, single fill colour. Keep the track light and the fill on-brand so completion reads at a glance.
  • Determinate only. These show a known percentage; for unknown waits use a spinner instead.
  • Accessible by role. Each maps to an ARIA progressbar with aria-valuenow/min/max.
In React

Props

// linear — labelPosition: right | bottom | top-floating | bottom-floating
<ProgressBar min={0} max={100} value={40} />
<ProgressBar labelPosition="right" min={0} max={100} value={40} />

// circle & half-circle — size: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg
<ProgressBarCircle size="sm" label="Active users" min={0} max={100} value={40} />
<ProgressBarHalfCircle size="md" min={0} max={100} value={40} />

Radio buttons

Free and open-source radio button components for modern apps and websites — built with React Aria for accessibility and styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗ React Aria ↗
Get started

Installation

Add this radio button component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add radio-buttons
// copy the component, then import
import { RadioButton, RadioGroup } from "@/components/base/radio-buttons";

Radio button examples

Below are examples and variations of this radio button component.

Example

Radio button example

A single-select group of pricing plans with a label and a hint beneath each option. Click any row — only one stays selected.

Variant

With label

Label only — no hint text.

Variant

With label and hint

Both a label and a supporting hint for extra context.

State

Disabled

The whole group is disabled via isDisabled on the RadioGroup — the selected value still shows, faded.

State

Disabled individual option

A single option is disabled via isDisabled on that RadioButton, while the rest stay interactive.

Size

Sizes

Two sizes via the size prop on the group: small (default) and medium for larger touch targets.

Small · default
Medium
Guidelines

Using radio buttons

  • One choice, always one selected. Radios are for mutually-exclusive options where a default makes sense — if a choice can be cleared, use a checkbox.
  • Wrap in a group. Every RadioButton lives inside a RadioGroup with an aria-label so screen readers announce the set.
  • Hints earn their place. Add a hint only when the label alone is ambiguous — like the plan limits here.
  • Disable the group or the option. Disable everything with isDisabled on the group, or a single row on the button.
  • Medium for prominence. Use size="md" on prominent forms or for larger touch targets.
In React

Props

// size: sm | md   ·   defaultValue for uncontrolled
<RadioGroup aria-label="Pricing plans" defaultValue="basic">
  <RadioButton label="Basic plan" hint="Up to 10 users and 20 GB data." value="basic" />
  <RadioButton label="Business plan" hint="Up to 20 users and 40 GB data." value="business" />
  <RadioButton isDisabled label="Enterprise plan" hint="Unlimited users and unlimited data." value="enterprise" />
</RadioGroup>

Radio groups

Powerful, customizable, fully-accessible selection groups for modern apps and websites — built on React Aria's RadioGroup pattern and styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE React Aria ↗
Get started

Installation

Add this radio groups component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add radio-groups
// copy the component, then import
import { RadioGroup, Radio } from "@/components/base/radio-groups";

Radio group examples

Six variants, each tuned to a different kind of choice. Every group is single-select — click to switch.

Variant
Get the code

Icon simple

Compact rows with a feature icon, an inline price, a description, and a checkbox indicator at the right. Selected rows get a brand border.

Variant
Get the code

Icon card

Taller cards leading with a large price, an optional promo badge, and the icon + name in a header row.

Variant
Get the code

Avatar

A people picker — avatar, name, @handle, and role/team. Ideal for assignees and reviewers.

Variant
Get the code

Payment icon

Saved payment methods with a brand slot, card details, expiry, and inline actions. The action buttons don't trigger selection.

Variant
Get the code

Radio button

The classic feel — a circular radio dot on the left, price inline, description below.

Variant
Get the code

Checkbox

Identical to radio button but with a square checkbox indicator. Still single-select — the checkbox look is purely stylistic.

Comparison

Choosing a variant

  • Icon simple — plan/tier lists where a feature icon and inline price are enough.
  • Icon card — pricing pages that need a prominent price and promo badges.
  • Avatar — assigning people: name, handle, role.
  • Payment icon — saved cards with expiry and per-item actions.
  • Radio button / Checkbox — the classic indicator on the left; the checkbox is single-select despite its look.
  • Don't mix indicator styles in one group, and switch to a Select past ~7–8 options.
Accessibility

Behaviour

  • Each group is a WAI-ARIA radiogroup with an aria-label ("Payment plans", "Users", "Payment options").
  • Arrow keys move between options, Space selects; exactly one stays selected.
  • Selected state is conveyed by both the brand border and the filled indicator — never colour alone.
In React

Props

// variant chooses the item layout; all are single-select
<RadioGroup aria-label="Payment plans" defaultValue="basic">
  <Radio value="basic" variant="icon-simple" icon={Package}
    label="Basic plan" price="$10/month"
    description="Includes up to 10 users, 20 GB…" />
  <Radio value="business" variant="icon-simple" icon={Building}
    label="Business plan" price="$20/month" description="…" />
</RadioGroup>

Select

Free and open-source select components for modern apps and websites — dropdown selection with labels, hints, icons, avatars, search, and tag multi-select. Built on React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗ React Aria ↗
Get started

Installation

Add this select component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add select
// copy the component files, then import
import { Select, type SelectItemType } from "@/components/base/select/select";
import { TagSelect } from "@/components/base/select/tag-select";

Select examples

Below are examples and variations of this select component. Click any trigger to open it.

Variant
Get the code

Default

Label with a required marker and tooltip, a placeholder trigger, a list of people with @handle supporting text, and a hint. Lana Steiner is disabled.

Phoenix Baker@phoenix

Abraham Baker@abraham
Adem Lane@adem
Jackson Reed@jackson
Jessie Meyton@jessie
This is a hint text to help user.
State
Get the code

Disabled

The whole select is disabled via isDisabled on the trigger.

Phoenix Baker@phoenix

Jessie Meyton@jessie

Size
Get the code

Sizes

Three sizes via the size prop — sm (default), md, and lg.

Phoenix Baker@phoenix

Get the code

Icon leading

A leading icon on the trigger and on every option.

Phoenix Baker@phoenix
Olivia Rhye@olivia

Avatar leading

Each option carries an avatar; the trigger shows a leading icon until one is picked.

PBPhoenix Baker@phoenix
