Get the code

Dot leading

A status dot indicator on the trigger and each option.

Phoenix Baker@phoenix
Olivia Rhye@olivia
Lana Steiner@lana
Demi Wilkinson@demi
Candice Wu@candice
Natali Craig@natali
Abraham Baker@abraham
Adem Lane@adem
Jackson Reed@jackson
Jessie Meyton@jessie
This is a hint text to help user.
Variant
Get the code

Search (ComboBox)

A text trigger that filters the list as you type, with a ⌘K shortcut hint. Try typing a name.

⌘K
Phoenix Baker@phoenix
Olivia Rhye@olivia
Lana Steiner@lana
Demi Wilkinson@demi
Candice Wu@candice
Natali Craig@natali
Abraham Baker@abraham
Adem Lane@adem
Jackson Reed@jackson
Jessie Meyton@jessie
This is a hint text to help user.
Variant
Get the code

Tags (multi-select)

Pick several — chosen people appear as removable tags inside the trigger. Click options to add, × to remove.

PBPhoenix Baker@phoenix
OROlivia Rhye@olivia
LSLana Steiner@lana
DWDemi Wilkinson@demi
CWCandice Wu@candice
NCNatali Craig@natali
ABAbraham Baker@abraham
ALAdem Lane@adem
JRJackson Reed@jackson
JMJessie Meyton@jessie
This is a hint text to help user.
Accessibility

Behaviour

  • Built on React Aria with full keyboard navigation and listbox / option / combobox roles.
  • Disabled items get aria-disabled and aren't focusable; required fields announce aria-required.
  • The tooltip adds an info icon with an accessible description; the label is programmatically associated.
FAQ

Common questions

  • What sizes are available? sm (default), md, and lg.
  • How do I disable the whole select? Add isDisabled to <Select>.
  • Disable one item? Set disabled: true on the item and pass isDisabled to <Select.Item>.
  • Search/filtering? Use <Select.ComboBox> instead of <Select>.
  • Icons or avatars? Pass icon (component/JSX) or avatarUrl on each item.
In React

Props

// size: sm | md | lg
<Select isRequired label="Team member" tooltip="This is a tooltip"
  hint="This is a hint text to help user." placeholder="Select team member" items={items}>
  {(item) => (
    <Select.Item id={item.id} supportingText={item.supportingText}
      isDisabled={item.isDisabled} icon={item.icon} avatarUrl={item.avatarUrl}>
      {item.label}
    </Select.Item>
  )}
</Select>

// search & multi-select variants
<Select.ComboBox label="Search" placeholder="Search" items={items}>…</Select.ComboBox>
<TagSelect label="Search" selectedItems={selectedItems} items={items}>…</TagSelect>

Multi-select

Free and open-source multi-select components for modern apps and websites — a searchable checklist with a selected-count badge and a select-all / reset footer. Built on React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗ React Aria ↗
Get started

Installation

Add this multi-select component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add select
// copy the files, then import
import { MultiSelect } from "@/components/base/select/multi-select";
import type { SelectItemType } from "@/components/base/select/select";

Multi-select examples

Below are examples and variations of this multi-select component. Open one, search, toggle teams, or use Select all / Reset.

Example
Get the code

Multi-select example

The trigger shows a selected-count badge plus supporting text (total users). Two teams are pre-selected — 2 selected, 16 users.

Engineering12 users
Design10 users
Product6 users
Marketing8 users
Sales12 users
Customer Success4 users
Operations2 users
Finance2 users
This is a hint text to help user.
Size
Get the code

Small

A compact trigger via size="sm" — for dense UIs and sidebars.

Engineering12 users
Design10 users
Product6 users
Marketing8 users
Sales12 users
Customer Success4 users
Operations2 users
Finance2 users
This is a hint text to help user.
Size
Get the code

Medium

The default size via size="md" — for most forms.

Engineering12 users
Design10 users
Product6 users
Marketing8 users
Sales12 users
Customer Success4 users
Operations2 users
Finance2 users
This is a hint text to help user.
Size
Get the code

Large

A bigger trigger via size="lg" — for prominent fields.

Engineering12 users
Design10 users
Product6 users
Marketing8 users
Sales12 users
Customer Success4 users
Operations2 users
Finance2 users
This is a hint text to help user.
State
Get the code

Disabled

Set isDisabled to mute the trigger and block interaction — shows the placeholder, no badge.

Engineering12 users
Design10 users
Product6 users
Marketing8 users
Sales12 users
Customer Success4 users
Operations2 users
Finance2 users
This is a hint text to help user.
Popover

Features

  • Search filters the list as you type (showSearch, on by default).
  • Select all / Reset live in the footer — wired via onSelectAll and onReset; hide the whole footer with showFooter={false}.
  • Supporting text next to the count comes from supportingText (e.g. "16 users"); customise the count itself with selectedCountFormatter.
  • Empty state title and description show when there are no matches (emptyStateTitle / emptyStateDescription).
Accessibility

Behaviour

  • Keyboard: arrow keys move, Enter/Space toggles, Escape closes.
  • Proper ARIA on the trigger, listbox, and each option; selection is a React Aria Selection (a Set of ids, or "all").
FAQ

Common questions

  • What sizes? sm, md (default), lg.
  • Disable it? isDisabled on <MultiSelect>.
  • Select all / Reset? Pass onSelectAll / onReset (footer buttons).
  • Supporting text by the count? The supportingText prop.
  • Hide the search? showSearch={false}.
  • Controlled vs uncontrolled? selectedKeys+onSelectionChange, or defaultSelectedKeys.
In React

Props

// size: sm | md | lg   ·   selection: Set of ids, or "all"
<MultiSelect isRequired size="md" label="Teams" tooltip="This is a tooltip"
  hint="This is a hint text to help user." placeholder="Select teams" items={teamItems}
  selectedKeys={selectedKeys} onSelectionChange={setSelectedKeys}
  supportingText={`${userCount} users`}
  onSelectAll={() => setSelectedKeys(new Set(teamItems.map((t) => t.id)))}
  onReset={() => setSelectedKeys(new Set())}>
  {(item) => (
    <MultiSelect.Item id={item.id} supportingText={item.supportingText}
      selectionIndicator="checkbox" selectionIndicatorAlign="left">
      {item.label}
    </MultiSelect.Item>
  )}
</MultiSelect>

Sliders

Free and open-source slider components for modern apps and websites — single value or range, with three label styles. Built on React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗ React Aria ↗
Get started

Installation

Add this slider component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add slider
// copy base/slider/slider.tsx, then import
import { Slider } from "@/components/base/slider/slider";
// peer dependency
npm install react-aria-components

Slider examples

Below are examples and variations of this slider component. Drag a thumb, or focus it and use the arrow keys.

Example
Get the code

Slider example

A two-thumb range slider with the selected range highlighted in brand. No visible labels by default.

0%
25%
Variant
Get the code

Default

The range slider, two thumbs at 0% and 25%. labelPosition defaults to hidden.

0%
25%
Variant
Get the code

Bottom label

The value sits below each thumb — always visible without covering the track. labelPosition="bottom".

0%
25%
Variant
Get the code

Top floating

A pill tooltip floats above each thumb — a modern, compact aesthetic. labelPosition="top-floating".

0%
25%
Variant
Get the code

Single thumb

One thumb for a single value — the fill runs from the left edge to the thumb. Pass a number, not an array.

50%
Customization

Formatting & range

  • Single vs range. Pass a number (defaultValue={50}) for one thumb, or an array ([0, 25]) for a range.
  • Label position. default hides it, bottom shows it under the thumb, top-floating shows a pill above.
  • Custom units. Override the percent default with formatOptions (currency, °C, …) or a labelFormatter like (v) => `$${v}`.
  • Step. Constrain values to meaningful increments with step.
Accessibility

Behaviour

  • Each thumb is role="slider" with aria-valuemin/max/now; arrow keys move by step, Home/End jump to the ends.
  • The focus ring shows on keyboard focus — never suppress it.
  • Provide an aria-label when there's no visible heading; keep the track at least 200px wide.
Resources

Design tokens

  • --track — unfilled track · --magenta — active fill.
  • --card — thumb background · shadow for depth · brand ring on focus.
  • Top-floating pill uses an inverse surface (dark on light, light on dark) for contrast.
In React

Props

// range with bottom labels
<Slider defaultValue={[0, 25]} labelPosition="bottom" />

// single value, floating pill, custom format
<Slider defaultValue={22} minValue={-10} maxValue={40} step={0.5}
  labelPosition="top-floating"
  formatOptions={{ style: "unit", unit: "celsius", maximumFractionDigits: 1 }} />

Tags

Free and open-source tag components for modern apps and websites — label, categorize, and filter, with avatars, dots, counts, dismissal, and selection. Built on React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗ React Aria ↗
Get started

Installation

Add this tags component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add tags
// copy base/tags/tags.tsx, then import
import { Tag, TagGroup, TagList } from "@/components/base/tags";

Tags examples

Below are examples and variations of this tags component. The selectable ones toggle on click; the × ones remove.

Example
Get the code

Tags example

The four decorators at a glance — plain label, flag avatar, person avatar, and a status dot.

Label
Label
ORLabel
Label
Size
Get the code

Sizes

Three sizes via the size prop on the group — sm, md (default), lg.

Label
Label
ORLabel
Label
Label
Label
ORLabel
Label
Label
Label
ORLabel
Label
Variant
Get the code

Close X

Pass onRemove on the group (or onClose per tag) to add a remove button. Click the × to dismiss a tag.

Label
Label
ORLabel
Label
Variant
Get the code

Count

A numeric badge after the label via the count prop — handy for item counts.

Label5
Label5
ORLabel5
Label5
Variant
Get the code

Checkbox

Selectable tags via selectionMode (single or multiple). Click to toggle — the first two start selected.

Label
Label
ORLabel
Label
Variant
Get the code

Checkbox + Close X

Selectable and dismissible together — toggle by clicking the body, remove with the ×.

Label
Label
ORLabel
Label
Variant
Get the code

Checkbox + Count

Selectable tags that also show a count.

Label5
Label5
ORLabel5
Label5
Decorators

Mix & match

  • Plain — text only. Flag / person avatar — avatarSrc (use avatarContrastBorder for light avatars). Dot — dot. Count — count={n}.
  • Any decorator combines with any variant — size, dismissible, selectable, or count.
  • Disable a single tag with isDisabled (muted, non-interactive).
Accessibility

Behaviour

  • Built on React Aria's TagGroup — arrow keys move between tags, Tab moves in/out.
  • Delete / Backspace removes the focused tag; Enter / Space toggles selection.
  • Remove buttons announce "Remove this tag"; disabled tags get aria-disabled.
FAQ

Common questions

  • Dismissible? Add onRemove on the group (or onClose per tag) — a × appears.
  • Selection? Set selectionMode to single or multiple with selectedKeys + onSelectionChange.
  • Sizes? sm, md (default), lg via size.
  • Avatars/dots/counts? avatarSrc, dot, count — combinable.
  • Disable one? isDisabled on the tag.
In React

Props

// selectable + dismissible group
<TagGroup label="Tags" size="md" selectionMode="multiple"
  selectedKeys={selected} onSelectionChange={setSelected}
  onRemove={(keys) => setTags(tags.filter((t) => !keys.has(t.id)))}>
  <TagList className="flex gap-4" items={tags}>
    {(item) => <Tag {...item} count={item.count}>{item.label}</Tag>}
  </TagList>
</TagGroup>

Toggle field

Free and open-source toggle field (switch) components for modern apps and websites — boolean on/off controls with optional label and hint. Built on React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗ React Aria ↗
Get started

Installation

Add this toggle component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add toggle
// copy base/toggle/toggle.tsx, then import
import { Toggle, ToggleBase } from "@/components/base/toggle";

Toggle examples

Below are examples and variations of this toggle component. Click any switch (or its label) to flip it.

Example
Get the code

Toggle example

A switch with a label and a hint — the classic "remember me" field.

Remember meSave my login details for next time.
Variant
Get the code

Base

Just the switch — the raw ToggleBase knob with no label.

Variant
Get the code

With label

A switch with a single label.

Remember me
Variant
Get the code

With label and hint

A label plus a supporting hint beneath it.

Remember meSave my login details for next time.
State
Get the code

Disabled

Set isDisabled to mute the switch and block interaction.

Remember meSave my login details for next time.
Size
Get the code

Sizes

Two sizes via the size prop — sm (default) and md.

Remember meSave my login details for next time.
Remember meSave my login details for next time.
Style
Get the code

Slim

The slim style — a thinner track with the knob riding on top. Set slim.

Style
Get the code

Slim with label and hint

The slim style with a label and hint.

Remember meSave my login details for next time.
Resources

Sizes reference

  • Default — sm: 36×20 track, 16px knob · md: 44×24 track, 20px knob.
  • Slim — sm: 32×16 track · md: 40×20 track; the knob matches the track height.
  • Off track uses a neutral fill; on switches to --magenta; the white knob keeps a soft shadow.
Accessibility

Behaviour

  • Built on React Aria's Switch — role="switch" with aria-checked kept in sync.
  • Toggle with Space/Enter when focused; the label and hint are associated with the control.
  • Disabled switches expose aria-disabled and don't respond to input.
In React

Props

// size: sm | md   ·   slim for the thin style
<Toggle size="sm" label="Remember me" hint="Save my login details for next time." />
<Toggle size="md" slim label="Remember me" />
<Toggle isDisabled label="Remember me" />

// raw knob only, for custom compositions
<ToggleBase size="md" isSelected />

Tooltips

Free and open-source tooltip components for modern apps and websites — non-interactive floating labels on hover or focus, with an optional arrow, supporting text, and 8 placements. Built on React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗ React Aria ↗
Get started

Installation

Add this tooltip component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add tooltip
// copy base/tooltip/tooltip.tsx, then import
import { Tooltip, TooltipTrigger } from "@/components/base/tooltip";

Tooltip examples

Below are examples and variations of this tooltip component. Hover (or keyboard-focus) a help icon to reveal its tooltip.

Example
Get the code

Tooltip example

Hover the help icon to see the default tooltip.

This is a tooltip
Variant
Get the code

Default

Tooltips show extra information on hover — no arrow, no description by default.

This is a tooltip
Variant
Get the code

With arrow

Pass the arrow boolean to add a pointer that rotates to match the placement.

This is a tooltip
Variant
Get the code

With supporting text

Pass a description to show secondary text below the title.

This is a tooltipTooltips are used to describe or identify an element. In most scenarios, tooltips help the user understand meaning, function or alt-text.
Placement
Get the code

Placements

Set the placement prop — React Aria supports all eight positions. Hover each icon.

This is a tooltipTop left
This is a tooltipTop
This is a tooltipTop right
This is a tooltipBottom left
This is a tooltipBottom
This is a tooltipBottom right
This is a tooltipLeft
This is a tooltipRight
Resources

Placement reference

  • Eight placements — top, top-left, top-right, bottom, bottom-left, bottom-right, left, right (default top).
  • offset sets the gap from the trigger (default 6px); crossOffset is auto-balanced for left/right-aligned placements.
  • delay (default 300ms) before showing; closeDelay (default 0) before hiding.
Accessibility

Behaviour

  • Built on React Aria's TooltipTrigger — shows on hover and keyboard focus, hides on blur or Escape.
  • The tooltip is role="tooltip" and non-interactive; the trigger is a real <button>.
  • Tooltips supplement — never put essential actions or long content inside one.
In React

Props

// arrow + description + placement
<Tooltip arrow placement="bottom"
  title="This is a tooltip"
  description="Tooltips help the user understand meaning, function or alt-text.">
  <TooltipTrigger>
    <HelpCircle className="size-4" />
  </TooltipTrigger>
</Tooltip>

Utility buttons

Free and open-source utility button components for modern apps and websites — small, icon-only actions (copy, download, delete, edit) plus a dedicated close button. Built on React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗ React Aria ↗
Get started

Installation

Add this utility button component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add button-utility
// icon-only action button + dedicated close button
import { ButtonUtility } from "@/components/base/buttons/button-utility";
import { CloseButton } from "@/components/base/buttons/close-button";

Utility button examples

Below are examples and variations of this utility button component. Each carries a tooltip — hover to see it.

Example
Get the code

Utility buttons example

Icon-only actions — copy, download, delete, edit — in the ghost (tertiary) style.

CopyDownloadDeleteEdit
Variant
Get the code

Secondary

A subtle border and a hover background — the most common style for toolbars, table rows, and card actions.

CopyDownloadDeleteEdit
Variant
Get the code

Tertiary

A ghost button — transparent at rest, background on hover. For low-prominence, inline actions.

CopyDownloadDeleteEdit
Size
Get the code

Sizes

Two sizes via the size prop — xs (compact) and sm (standard), shown in the secondary style.

CopyDownloadDeleteEdit
CopyDownloadDeleteEdit
State
Get the code

Disabled

Set isDisabled to mute the buttons and block interaction.

CopyDownloadDeleteEdit
Close
Get the code

Close X

The dedicated CloseButton for modals, drawers, toasts, and banners — light theme, three sizes.

Close
Get the code

Close X dark

The same close button tuned for dark surfaces via theme="dark".

Resources

Reference

  • ButtonUtility — color secondary or tertiary; size xs or sm; pass an icon and a tooltip.
  • CloseButton — a separate component; theme light or dark; size sm, md, lg.
  • Tertiary uses tighter spacing (4px) than secondary (8px); icon-only buttons rely on tooltips for clarity.
Accessibility

Behaviour

  • Every icon-only button needs an accessible name — supplied here via aria-label and the visible tooltip.
  • Disabled uses React Aria's isDisabled (correct ARIA + keyboard handling), not the native attribute.
  • Hover and keyboard focus both reveal the tooltip; the close button announces "Close".
In React

Props

// icon-only action buttons
<ButtonUtility size="sm" color="secondary" tooltip="Copy" icon={Copy01} />
<ButtonUtility size="xs" color="tertiary" tooltip="Edit" icon={Edit01} />
<ButtonUtility isDisabled color="secondary" tooltip="Delete" icon={Trash01} />

// dedicated close button
<CloseButton size="md" theme="light" />
<CloseButton size="md" theme="dark" />

Verification code inputs

Free and open-source verification code (OTP / PIN) input components for modern apps and websites — individual digit slots with auto-advance and optional separators. Built on Input OTP, styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗ Input OTP ↗
Get started

Installation

Add this verification code input component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add pin-input
// built on the input-otp library
import { PinInput } from "@/components/base/pin-input";

Verification code input examples

Below are examples and variations of this verification code input component. Type to auto-advance; Backspace steps back.

Example
Get the code

Verification code input example

A six-digit code split into two groups by a separator.

–
This is a hint text to help user.
Variant
Get the code

Four digits

A compact four-digit code with no separator.

This is a hint text to help user.
Variant
Get the code

With separator

Six digits grouped 3 + 3 with a separator between.

–
This is a hint text to help user.
State
Get the code

Disabled

Set disabled to mute the slots and block input.

This is a hint text to help user.
Size
Get the code

Sizes

Slots scale with the size prop — sm, md, lg.

This is a hint text to help user.
This is a hint text to help user.
This is a hint text to help user.
Anatomy

Structure

  • PinInput wraps PinInput.Group (the slots), with optional PinInput.Separator, plus PinInput.Label and PinInput.Description.
  • Each PinInput.Slot is one digit; the placeholder "0" shows until a value is entered.
  • Sizes range from xxxs to lg; numeric keyboards via inputmode="numeric".
Accessibility

Behaviour

  • Typing a digit moves focus to the next slot; Backspace on an empty slot steps to the previous one.
  • Pasting a full code distributes digits across the slots (in the React component); each slot has an accessible label.
  • Disabled slots are non-interactive and visually muted.
In React

Props

// size: xxxs | xxs | xs | sm | md | lg
<PinInput size="md">
  <PinInput.Label>Secure code</PinInput.Label>
  <PinInput.Group>
    <PinInput.Slot index={0} />
    <PinInput.Slot index={1} />
    <PinInput.Slot index={2} />
    <PinInput.Separator />
    <PinInput.Slot index={3} />
    <PinInput.Slot index={4} />
    <PinInput.Slot index={5} />
  </PinInput.Group>
  <PinInput.Description>This is a hint text to help user.</PinInput.Description>
</PinInput>

Video players

Powerful and customizable video player components for modern apps and websites — a poster, a play overlay, and a controls bar in three sizes. Built with React, styled with Tailwind CSS.

Get started

Installation

Add this video player component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add video-player
// copy base/video-player/video-player.tsx, then import
import { VideoPlayer } from "@/components/base/video-player";

Video player examples

Below are examples and variations of this video player component. Click the play button to start — click again to pause.

Example
Get the code

Video player example

The default player with a poster and a centered play button. Hover to reveal the controls bar.

0:002:34
Size
Get the code

Video player sm

A compact player for cards and sidebars.

0:002:34
Size
Get the code

Video player md

The standard player for most content areas.

0:002:34
Size
Get the code

Video player lg

A large player for feature sections and heroes.

0:002:34
Anatomy

Structure

  • Poster — the still frame shown before playback (set via a poster / src thumbnail).
  • Play overlay — the centered button; it fades out once playback starts.
  • Controls bar — play/pause, elapsed and total time, and a scrubber that fills as the video plays.
  • Three sizes — sm, md, lg — all locked to a 16:9 frame.
In React

Props

// size: sm | md | lg
<VideoPlayer
  size="md"
  src="/videos/intro.mp4"
  poster="/images/intro-poster.jpg"
/>

Activity feeds

Powerful and customizable activity feed components for modern apps and websites — chronological event streams and direct-message previews in divided, connected, and spaced layouts. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

Example
Get the code

Activity feed example

The default feed combines activity events and message previews in one list, separated by dividers — covering every event type from file uploads to invites.

Get started

Installation

Some activity feed components depend on other components (avatars, badges, file icons) to function properly. Before using them, add the required components via the CLI.

npx tungo-ui@latest add activity-feed
// also install its dependencies
npx tungo-ui@latest add avatar badge file-icon

Activity feed examples

Below are examples and variations of this activity feed component — the same items rendered in three layouts.

Layout
Get the code

Activity feed divided

Items separated by a horizontal divider with a compact gap.

Rene Wells9:24am 20 Jan 2027
@rene
Hey @olivia—just wanted to say thanks for your help on this. Really buried under!
Anatomy

Item types

  • File upload — action text + a file card (type badge, name, size).
  • Direct message (text) — “Sent you a message” + a message bubble.
  • Direct message (file) — “Sent you a file” + a file card.
  • Comment, Added to project, Task creation, Project creation — action text + a project link.
  • Labels added — action text + colored label chips.
  • Team invitation (sent / received) — action text + a person link.
  • Each item: avatar (with optional online dot), name, timestamp, action, and optional attachment.

Alerts

Powerful and customizable alert components for modern apps and websites — compact floating cards and full-width banners, in six semantic colors. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

Example
Get the code

Alert example

A floating, card-style alert with a status icon, bold title and supporting description.

We’ve just released a new feature
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipisicing elit. Aliquid pariatur, ipsum dolor.
Get started

Installation

You can add this alerts component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add alerts
// copy base/alert/alert.tsx, then import
import { Alert } from "@/components/base/alert";

Alert examples

Below are examples and variations of this alert component — floating cards and full-width banners across six colors.

Floating
Get the code

Floating default

Neutral gray info icon — the standard floating alert.

We’ve just released a new feature
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipisicing elit. Aliquid pariatur, ipsum dolor.
Floating
Get the code

Floating brand

Brand-tinted info icon for product announcements.

We’ve just released a new feature
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipisicing elit. Aliquid pariatur, ipsum dolor.
Floating
Get the code

Floating gray

A muted dark-gray treatment.

We’ve just released a new feature
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipisicing elit. Aliquid pariatur, ipsum dolor.
Floating
Get the code

Floating error

Destructive red — for failed actions and problems.

There was a problem with that action
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipisicing elit. Aliquid pariatur, ipsum dolor.
Floating
Get the code

Floating warning

Cautionary amber — for things to be aware of.

Just to let you know this might be a problem
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipisicing elit. Aliquid pariatur, ipsum dolor.
Floating
Get the code

Floating success

Positive green — for confirmations.

We’ve just released a new feature
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipisicing elit. Aliquid pariatur, ipsum dolor.
Full-width
Get the code

Full-width default

Banner spanning the container, with inline text, Dismiss, a CTA and a close button.

Successfully updated profile Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur.
Full-width
Get the code

Full-width brand

Brand banner for announcements.

We’ve just released a new feature Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur.
Full-width
Get the code

Full-width gray

Muted gray banner.

We’ve just released a new feature Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur.
Full-width
Get the code

Full-width error

Destructive banner with a “Learn more” CTA.

There was a problem with that action Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur.
Full-width
Get the code

Full-width warning

Cautionary banner with a “Learn more” CTA.

Just to let you know this might be a problem Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur.
Full-width
Get the code

Full-width success

Positive banner confirming an action.

Successfully updated profile Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur.
Anatomy

Structure

  • Floating — status icon + bold title + description in a bordered card, centered, no actions.
  • Full-width — status icon + inline title and description, then Dismiss, a solid brand CTA (View changes / Learn more), and a close (×) button.
  • Six colors — default, brand, gray, error, warning, success — each pairing an icon (info / alert / check circle) with its semantic tint.
  • Dismiss and the close button both remove the alert.

Breadcrumbs

Powerful and customizable breadcrumb components for modern apps and websites — text, line, button and account-aware variants with chevron or slash separators, plus built-in overflow handling. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

React Aria ↗
Example
Get the code

Breadcrumbs example

The default breadcrumb, previewing both separator styles — chevron and slash — with a collapsed ellipsis item and a brand current page.

Settings…Team
/Settings/…/Team
Get started

Installation

You can add this breadcrumbs component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add breadcrumbs
// copy base/breadcrumbs/breadcrumbs.tsx, then import
import { Breadcrumbs } from "@/components/base/breadcrumbs";

Breadcrumbs examples

Below are examples and variations of this breadcrumbs component.

Variant
Get the code

Breadcrumbs text

Plain text links with chevron and slash separators. The current page is in brand color.

Settings…Team
/Settings/…/Team
Variant
Get the code

Breadcrumbs text with line

Text links with an underline treatment, in both separator styles.

Settings…Team
/Settings/…/Team
Variant
Get the code

Breadcrumbs button

Each item is a button with hover pills; the current page sits in a filled gray pill.

Settings…Team
/Settings/…/Team
Account
Get the code

Account button chevron

A workspace-aware breadcrumb — app logo, a user account button with a dropdown caret, and the current page — with chevron separators.

WarpspeedSettings
Account
Get the code

Account button slash

The same account breadcrumb with slash separators.

Warpspeed//Settings
Anatomy

Structure

  • Root — a home icon (no label) or a workspace logo + name.
  • Text links — intermediate pages like “Settings”.
  • Ellipsis — a “…” item that stands in for collapsed/hidden levels; root and current always stay visible.
  • Current page — the last item, marked aria-current="page", non-interactive.
  • Account button — the user item is a <button> with an avatar and a selector caret for switching context.
  • Separators come in two styles — chevron (>) and slash (/).

Calendars

Powerful and customizable calendar components for modern apps and websites — month, week and day views with a shared toolbar, color-coded events, a mini date picker and an event detail panel. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

Example
Get the code

Calendar example

The month view — a full June 2026 grid with color-coded events, today highlighted, and overflow handling.

June 2026Week 3
Jun 1, 2026 – Jun 30, 2026

Get started

Installation

You can add this calendar component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add calendar
// copy base/calendar/calendar.tsx, then import
import { Calendar } from "@/components/base/calendar";

Calendar examples

Product demo
Friday, Jan 10, 2027
1:30 PM – 3:30 PM
10 min before
SRODCKAWELOR
6 guests·5 yes·1 awaiting
About this event

Sienna is inviting you to a scheduled Zoom meeting.

Topic: Product demo for the new dashboard and Q&A session.

Join Zoom Meeting: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/86341969512

Meeting ID: 863 4196 9512

Anatomy

Structure

  • Toolbar — date badge, month + week, range, search, prev/Today/next, a view selector and the brand “Add event” CTA.
  • Month — 7-column grid, today in a filled circle, color-coded event chips, all-day bars and “X more…” overflow.
  • Week / Day — hourly time grid with event blocks positioned by start time and scaled to duration, plus a current-time line.
  • Day sidebar — a mini month calendar with event dots and a detail panel (date, time, reminder, guests, description, links).
  • Six event colors — purple, blue, pink, green, yellow, orange — plus muted gray for out-of-month days.

Card headers

Powerful and customizable card header components for modern apps and websites — a title with an optional badge, description or avatar, plus secondary / primary actions and an overflow menu. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

Example
Get the code

Card header example

The base card header — title, badge, description, and right-aligned actions, sitting above a divider.

Team members10/20 seats
Manage your team members and their account permissions here.
Get started

Installation

These card header components don’t require any installation — just copy and paste them into your project once you’ve set up Tungo UI. Check out our installation guide to get started.

Card header examples

Below are examples and variations of this card header component.

Variant
Get the code

Card header with badge

A title paired with an outlined metadata badge (e.g. quota usage) and a supporting description.

Team members10/20 seats
Manage your team members and their account permissions here.
Variant
Get the code

Card header with avatar and badge

A user-centric header — avatar, name with a status badge, and a secondary line (email).

OR
Olivia RhyeNew user
olivia@untitledui.com
Anatomy

Structure

  • Title (required) — the bold heading; pair with an optional inline badge for status, count or plan.
  • Description or, in the avatar variant, an avatar + name + sub-label (email / role).
  • Actions — a secondary (outlined) and primary (brand) button, plus an overflow ⋮ menu for tertiary or destructive actions.
  • Divider — a horizontal rule separating the header from the card content below.
  • Keep to a single badge and at most two buttons + overflow; always include the title.

Color pickers

Powerful and customizable color picker components for modern apps and websites — a full canvas picker, swatch grids, brand and gray palettes, standalone hue / transparency sliders and a dense palette picker. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

Example
Get the code

Color picker example

The flagship picker — Solid / Gradient / Image tabs, a saturation canvas, a hue slider, a hex field with format and opacity, and saved swatches.

Saved
Stops
Saved
or drag and drop
Exposure
Contrast
Saturation
Temperature
Tint
Highlights
Shadows
Get started

Installation

You can add this color picker component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add color-picker
// compound component
import { ColorPicker } from "@/components/base/color-picker";

Color picker examples

Below are examples and variations of this color picker component. Drag the canvas and sliders, or tap a swatch.

Variant
Get the code

Color picker

The full picker with an eye-dropper, canvas, hue slider, hex field and saved colors.

Saved
Variant
Get the code

Color swatch picker

A large preview over a grid of preset swatches with a hex field — quick selection from a fixed set.

# FF1E9A
Variant
Get the code

Saved swatches small

A compact two-row preset grid (neutrals + accents) with a “Custom” hex input.

Custom
Variant
Get the code

Saved swatches medium

A saved-colors panel with a header, Add button, two swatch rows and a hex / opacity row.

Saved
Variant
Get the code

Brand swatches

A curated brand palette based on Tailwind CSS v4.2, with Docs and Reset actions.

BrandTailwind CSS v4.2
Variant
Get the code

Gray swatches

A single-row neutral palette from the Tailwind CSS v4.2 gray scale.

GrayTailwind CSS v4.2
Variant
Get the code

Minimal picker

A compact picker — eye-dropper, hue and transparency sliders, and a hex field. No canvas.

Variant
Get the code

Hue slider

A standalone hue slider with a live swatch preview.

100%
Variant
Get the code

Transparency slider

A standalone alpha slider over a checkered background to visualise opacity.

100%
Variant
Get the code

Palette picker

The full color palette in a dense grid, with an opacity slider, hex field and saved colors.

Opacity
Saved

Command menus

Powerful and customizable command menu (⌘K palette) components for modern apps and websites — keyboard-driven search over actions, users and integrations, in inline, stacked and split-panel layouts. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

React Aria ↗
Example
Get the code

Command menu example

The default palette — a search field over grouped actions with keyboard-shortcut badges.

⌘/
Marketing site redesign
New document⌘N
Invite colleagues⌘I
My profile⌘KP
Team profile⌘KT
Invite colleagues⌘I
Create new project⌘N
Support⌘H
Changelog⌘C
Get started

Installation

You can add this command menu component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add command-menu
// opens with ⌘/ by default
import { CommandMenu } from "@/components/base/command-menu";

Command menu examples

Below are examples and variations of this command menu component.

Get the code

A user search with Recent and All-users sections, plus a keyboard-hint footer.

⌘/
Integrations
G
GitHubgithub.com

Linearlinear.app
F
Figmafigma.com
Z
Zapierzapier.com

Notionnotion.so
S
Slackslack.com
L
Linear

Linear helps streamline software projects, sprints, tasks, and bug tracking.

Anatomy

Structure

  • Search input with a ⌘/ badge, opening the palette.
  • Grouped list — optional section labels (Recent, All users, Integrations, Designers), rows with a leading icon/avatar, label, optional description, and shortcut badges.
  • Inline vs stacked rows — single-line, or two-line with a description / handle.
  • Split layout — a left list paired with a right preview card (user profile or integration detail).
  • Empty state and a keyboard-hint footer (navigate / select / close / return to parent).

Content dividers

Powerful and customizable content divider components for modern apps and websites — a horizontal rule with a centered label, button, button group or icon, in single-line, dual-line and background-fill styles. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

Example
Get the code

Content divider example

A single-line divider with a centered label — ideal for date or section groupings in feeds.

Notifications
Get started

Installation

You can add this content dividers component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add content-divider
// copy base/content-divider/content-divider.tsx, then import
import { ContentDivider } from "@/components/base/content-divider";

Content dividers examples

Below are examples and variations of this content dividers component — each line style supports five content slots: label, button, navigation group, filter group, and icon button.

Line style
Get the code

Single line

Content sits over a single rule, masking the line behind it.

Notifications
Today
Line style
Get the code

Dual line

Content sits between two rule segments.

Notifications
Today
Line style
Get the code

Background fill

Labels render as filled pills; the rule runs behind the content.

Notifications
Today
Anatomy

Content slots

  • Label — a centered text label (e.g. “Today”, “Notifications”) for date / category groupings.
  • Button — a single inline action (e.g. “Add”).
  • Navigation group — previous / add / next icon buttons for inline paging.
  • Filter group — a segmented radio group (View all / Active / Inactive).
  • Icon button — a single icon-only action.
  • Any slot combines with any of the three line styles — 15 combinations in total.

Date pickers

Free and open-source date picker components for modern apps and websites — single date, date-time and range pickers, inline calendars, calendar cards and a full range card with presets. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗ React Aria ↗
Example
Get the code

Date picker example

A trigger button showing the selected date; opens a calendar popover.

Get started

Installation

You can add this date picker component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add date-picker
// copy base/date-picker/date-picker.tsx, then import
import { DatePicker } from "@/components/base/date-picker";

Date picker examples

Below are examples and variations of this date picker component. Click a day in the inline calendars to select it.

A single-date trigger.

Get the code

A trigger for a start–end range.

6 / 8 / 202612 : 00 AM–6 / 15 / 202612 : 00 AM
Anatomy

Structure

  • Trigger — a calendar-icon button displaying the current date / range, opening a popover.
  • Calendar — month header with prev/next, a manual MM/DD/YYYY input, a Today shortcut, and a Su–Sa day grid.
  • Selection — today shows a dot; the selected day is a filled circle; ranges fill a band between two filled endpoints.
  • Card variants add Cancel / Apply; the range card adds a 9-item preset sidebar and start/end datetime inputs.

Empty states

Powerful and customizable empty state components for modern apps and websites — for no-results, zero-data and onboarding moments, with featured-icon, illustration, file-icon and avatar treatments. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

GitHub ↗ React Aria ↗
Example
Get the code

Empty state example

No projects found

Your search “Landing page design” did not match any projects. Please try again.

Get started

Installation

You can add this empty states component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add empty-state
// copy base/empty-state/empty-state.tsx, then import
import { EmptyState } from "@/components/base/empty-state";

Empty states examples

Below are examples and variations of this empty states component:

Variant
Get the code

Featured icon

No projects found

Your search “Landing page design” did not match any projects. Please try again.

Variant
Get the code

Illustration

No projects found

Your search “Landing page design” did not match any projects. Please try again.

Variant
Get the code

File icon

No projects found

Your search “Landing page design” did not match any projects. Please try again.

Variant
Get the code

Avatar radius

AMKPRSTLJDCKORLWBM

No users found

Your search did not match any users. Please try again.

Variant
Get the code

Avatar row

AMKPRS
CKJDLW

Invite your first user

Add your team members and external users.

Variant
Get the code

Avatar grid

Sorry, no results!

We couldn’t find any apps or commands. Please try again or browse all apps.

Anatomy

Structure

  • Media — a featured icon, illustration, file icon, or avatar arrangement that sets the tone.
  • Heading (required) — a short label describing the state (e.g. “No projects found”).
  • Description — one or two lines of supporting context, often echoing the search term.
  • Actions — a secondary reset (Clear search / Create link) plus a primary forward action (New project / Add user / Integration).

File uploaders

Free and open-source file upload components for modern apps and websites — with drag-and-drop, click-to-upload, type filtering, size limits, live progress, error handling and file removal. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗
Example
Get the code

File upload example

TS
chatgpt-clone.ts
MP4

Get started

Installation

You can add this file upload component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add file-upload-base
// copy base/file-upload/file-upload-base.tsx, then import
import { FileUpload } from "@/components/base/file-upload";

PNG

invoice.pdf
Click to upload or drag and drop

Variant
Get the code

Max size limit

JPG
smaller_file.jpg
MP4
too_large.mp4
PDF
still_too_large.pdf
Click to upload or drag and drop
Upload files to add to this project (max. 1 MB).
Anatomy

States

  • Drop zone — a click / drag target with an upload-cloud icon, a “Click to upload” link, and a hint describing accepted types and limits.
  • Uploading (0–99%) — a spinner with “Uploading…” and a progress bar at the current percent.
  • Complete (100%) — a green check with “Complete” and a full bar.
  • Failed — a red row with “Failed” (or the full “Upload failed, please try again” sentence in the fill style) and a Try again action.
  • Progress fill overlays a translucent fill behind the row instead of a separate bar; Disabled dims the drop zone.

Filter bars

Powerful and customizable filter bar components for modern apps and websites — compose search, tabs, dropdown selects, date-range pickers, advanced query rows, dropdown menus and slideout panels. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

Example
Get the code

Filter bar example

⌘K
Get started

Installation

You can add this filter bar component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add filter-bar
// copy base/filter-bar/filter-bar.tsx, then import
import { FilterBar } from "@/components/base/filter-bar";

Filter bar examples

Below are examples and variations of this filter bar component:

Variant
Get the code

Simple

⌘K
Variant
Get the code

Tabs and search

Tabs for quick category switching alongside a search input for filtering within each category.

⌘K
Variant
Get the code

Tabs and date picker

Tab navigation for time-period switching plus a date-range picker for custom ranges.

Variant
Get the code

Date filters

Multiple time-range methods in one bar: a quick-select button, a period dropdown, and a date-range picker.

⌘K
Variant
Get the code

Dropdowns

Inline dropdown selects for key data dimensions.

Search for order
⌘K
Status
Category
Variant
Get the code

Dropdowns and date picker

Multiple dropdowns combined with a date-range picker for richer filter sets.

Sort by
Status
Teams
Variant
Get the code

Advanced filter inactive

A query-builder interface with a single empty row, ready to configure.

Variant
Get the code

Advanced filter active

Multiple active filter rows; each row can be configured and removed independently.

Filter dropdown menus

A Filters button that opens an advanced filter panel — click each trigger to open it.

Variant
Get the code

Filter dropdown empty state

Filters
Add filters to narrow down results.
No filters applied
Variant
Get the code

Filter dropdown inactive

Filters
Add filters to narrow down results.
Variant
Get the code

Filter dropdown active

Filters
Add filters to narrow down results.

Filter slideout menus

A full-height slideout panel for advanced filtering over a page.

Variant
Get the code

Filters advanced empty state menu

Filters
Add filters to narrow down results.
No filters applied
Variant
Get the code

Filters advanced active menu

Filters
Add filters to narrow down results.
FAQs

Frequently asked questions

A filter bar is composed of a root container plus any mix of search input, tabs, dropdown selects, a date-range picker, a filters button or icon button, and advanced filter rows — each independently optional.

Combine the filters icon button with one or more filter rows. Each row pairs a field selector, an operator selector and a value input, plus a remove control, with Clear all and Apply filters actions.

Yes — the Tabs and search and Tabs and date picker variants show tabs alongside a search input or date-range picker in the same bar.

The bar content wraps onto multiple lines when horizontal space is insufficient, and several controls collapse to compact icon-only buttons on small screens.

Drop a search field into the bar; it carries a search icon, placeholder text and an optional ⌘K shortcut hint, and can sit beside tabs, dropdowns or date pickers.

Header navigations

Free and open-source header navigation components for modern apps and websites — a single-tier Simple header and a two-tier Dual-tier header with primary nav, utility actions, a CTA and a contextual sub-nav. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗
Example
Get the code

Header navigation example

Tungo
CK
⌘K
Get started

Installation

You can add this header navigation component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add header-navigation
// copy base/app-navigation/header-navigation.tsx, then import
import { HeaderNavigation } from "@/components/base/app-navigation";

Header navigation examples

Below are examples and variations of this header navigation component. Click a nav link to switch the active page.

Variant
Get the code

Simple

A single-tier header: logo, primary nav links, and utility actions (search, settings, notifications, account).

Tungo
Variant
Get the code

Dual-tier

A two-tier header: tier one carries the logo, primary nav, an Upgrade CTA, utility icons and avatar; tier two carries a contextual sub-nav and a search bar.

Tungo
CK
⌘K
Anatomy

Structure

  • Logo — the product mark and wordmark at the far left.
  • Primary nav — the main destinations (Home, Dashboard, Projects, Tasks, Reporting, Users) with the current page marked active.
  • Utility actions — search, settings and a notifications bell (with a count badge), plus an Account button or avatar.
  • CTA — an “Upgrade now” button (Dual-tier tier one).
  • Sub-nav — a contextual second tier (Overview, Notifications, Analytics…) with an inline search field (Dual-tier tier two).

Inline CTAs

Powerful and customizable inline CTA components for modern apps and websites — compact in-product cards for announcements, subscriptions, plan changes, upgrades, payment methods and receipts. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

Example
Get the code

Inline CTA example

We’ve just released a new update!

Check out the all new dashboard view. Pages and now load faster.

Get started

Installation

These inline CTA components don’t require any installation — just copy and paste them into your project once you’ve set up Tungo UI. See the installation guide to get started.

Inline CTA examples

Below are examples and variations of this inline CTA component:

Variant
Get the code

Actions

We’ve just released a new update!

Check out the all new dashboard view. Pages and now load faster.

Variant
Get the code

Email field

We’ve just released a new update!

Check out the all new dashboard view. Pages and now load faster.

Subscribe to updates

Get the code

Change plan

Change your plan

Flexible pricing that grows with you.

Basic plan
$10 per month

Includes up to 10 users, 20 GB individual data and access to all features.

Variant
Get the code

Upgrade plan

Upgrade your plan

Need more space? Upgrade your plan today.

10 users
Add up to 10 team members.
20 GB data

All features

Variant

Payment method

Payment method

Change how you pay for your plan.

Card ending in 1234
Expiry 06/2028
billing@tungo.app
Variant
Get the code

Receipt

Your latest receipt is available

Download receipt for January 2027.

PDF
Receipt_January_2027.pdf
200 KB
Anatomy

Structure

  • Heading + supporting text — a short headline and one line of context.
  • Optional media — an illustration or image (as in the hero example).
  • Optional content — an email field, a plan summary, a feature checklist, a payment row, or a file row.
  • Actions — a secondary action (Dismiss / Learn more / All plans / Download) and a primary action (Changelog / Subscribe / Upgrade plan / View).

Loading indicators

Free and open-source loading indicator components for modern apps and websites — a line spinner with track, a bare line spinner and a dot-circle spinner, with optional labels and a full range of sizes. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

FREE GitHub ↗
Example
Get the code

Loading indicator example

Loading…
Loading…
Loading…
Get started

Installation

You can add this loading indicator component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add loading-indicator
// copy base/loading-indicator/loading-indicator.tsx, then import
import { LoadingIndicator } from "@/components/base/loading-indicator";

Loading indicator examples

Below are examples and variations of this loading indicator component:

Variant
Get the code

Variant
Get the code

Loading…

Line spinner

Variant
Get the code

Line spinner with label

Loading…
Variant
Get the code

Variant
Get the code

Dot circle with label

Loading…
Variant
Get the code

Sizes

Loading…
Loading…
Loading…
Loading…
Anatomy

Variants

  • Line simple — a faint full track ring with a rotating brand arc.
  • Line spinner — a bare rotating arc with no track.
  • Dot circle — a ring of dots with a rotating opacity comet.
  • Label — an optional “Loading…” caption below the spinner.
  • Sizes — each variant scales from small to extra-large, with the label scaling alongside.

Messaging

Powerful and customizable messaging components for modern apps and websites — a full chat UI with text, files, audio, images, link previews, reactions, replies, delivery status, typing indicators and rich compose bars. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

GitHub ↗
Example
Get the code

Messaging example

LS
Lana SteinerThursday 11:43am
Hey team, I’ve finished with the requirements doc!
LS
Lana SteinerThursday 11:43am
PDF
Tech requirements.pdf
1.2 MB
YouThursday 11:46am
Awesome! Thanks.
DW
Demi WilkinsonThursday 11:46am
Good timing—was just looking at this.
Today
PB
Phoenix BakerThursday 2:20pm
Hey Olivia, can you please review the latest design when you can?
You2:24pm
Sure thing, I’ll have a look today.
Get started

Installation

The messaging component depends on other components to function properly. Before using it, add the required components via the CLI:

npx tungo-ui@latest add messaging
// copy base/messaging/* then import
import { Message, MessageStatus, MessageAction } from "@/components/base/messaging";
Status
Get the code

Message status

Use the MessageStatus component to show delivery state — sent (single tick), read (brand double tick) or failed (red warning). Pass readAt to show when a message was read.

!

Modals

Powerful and customizable modal (dialog) components for modern apps and websites — overlays that capture attention for a single task, confirmation, or piece of information. Stacked, centered and horizontal layouts; default, warning, destructive, success and info intents; and a full catalogue of 50+ ready-made content patterns. Built with React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS.

Example
Get the code

Modal example

The flagship pattern — a centered featured icon, title, supporting text and a stacked action pair. This is the default onboarding / welcome dialog.

Welcome to Tungo
We’re glad you’re here. Take a quick tour to set up your workspace, or jump straight in and explore on your own.
Get started

Installation

You can add this modal component with the CLI, or copy it in manually.

npx tungo-ui@latest add modal
// npm install react-aria-components, then copy the compound component
import { DialogTrigger, ModalOverlay, Modal, Dialog, CloseButton } from "@/components/application/modal";

Modal examples

Below are examples and variations of this modal component, organised by layout, intent and content type. Every variant is shown open on its backdrop.

Layout
Get the code

Stacked left aligned

The default pattern — featured icon, title, supporting text and buttons stacked vertically with left alignment.

Delete file?
Are you sure you want to delete Q4-report.pdf? This action cannot be undone.
Layout
Get the code

Stacked centered

All content centred horizontally. Used for notifications, success states and media modals.

Payment successful
Your payment has been processed. A receipt has been sent to your email.
Layout
Get the code

Horizontal

Icon sits to the left of the title and description in a row; buttons sit below in the footer.

Unsaved changes
You have unsaved changes that will be lost if you leave this page. Save them before continuing?
Intent
Get the code

Warning

Caution-required actions use an amber featured icon.

Revoke API key?
Applications using this key will immediately lose access. You can generate a new key afterwards.
Intent
Get the code

Destructive

Irreversible actions use a red featured icon and a red primary button, often with a confirmation field.

Delete workspace
This permanently deletes Acme Inc. and all of its data for every member. This cannot be undone.
Intent
Get the code

Success

Completion confirmations use a green featured icon.

All set!
Your account has been verified and your trial is now active.
Intent
Get the code

Info

Informational dialogs use a blue/aqua featured icon.

New version available
Tungo 2.0 introduces a redesigned editor and faster sync. Release notes are available in the changelog.

New message
Messaging
Get the code
