Revenue
$48,768
Customers
2,420

Anatomy & guidance

How a 404 section is composed, the component matrix, and the rules that keep these pages helpful rather than dead ends.

Component matrix

The fourteen variants

VariantBadgeCTA buttonsResourcesSearchVisual
Simple 01Text labelGo back + Take me home———
Simple 02Dot pillGo back + Go home3 text links——
Simple 03—Go back + Take me home——Grid bg + icon
Simple 04Text labelGo back + Go home3 cards——
Simple 05—Go back + Go home—✓Ghost 404 text
Simple 06Dot pillGo back + Go home3 stacked rows—Grid bg
Split image 01———✓Abstract 3D glass
Split image 02—Go back + Take me home——Portrait photo
Split image 03———✓Gradient abstract
Split image 04Dot pillGo back + Go home——Scenic photo
Split image 05Text label—3 text links✓Metallic abstract
Illustration 01Text labelGo back + Take me home——SVG bubbles
Illustration 02Text label——✓SVG 404 outline
Screen mockupText labelGo back + Go home——Dashboard mockup
Theming

Dark mode & tokens

Every variant adapts to the active theme; the source’s purple brand maps to Tungo’s magenta.

Badge text / primary button / links--magenta
Page / canvas surface--canvas
Card / input / icon surface--card
Heading text--txt
Body text--txt-2
Border / grid pattern--hair
Responsive

Responsive behaviour

  • Simple and centered variants are single-column — only padding and heading size change.
  • Split, illustration and mockup variants hide the right panel below the large breakpoint, so the content column fills the width and there is no horizontal page scroll.
  • The grid background and ghost “404” are decorative; the grid is hidden on mobile to reduce noise.
  • Resource cards collapse from a three-column row to a single stacked column on small screens.
Guidelines

Do’s and don’ts

Do
  • Always give at least one path home — Go back or Take me home.
  • Keep the message clear, brief and free of jargon.
  • Offer a search field or helpful links when you can.
  • Reserve “Under maintenance” (Illustration 02, Screen mockup) for genuine downtime.
  • Match the heading voice to your brand.
Don’t
  • Leave the visitor stranded with no action.
  • Blame the user or use cryptic error codes alone.
  • Use the maintenance variants for a generic 404.
  • Hide the primary action below a tall illustration on mobile.
  • Remove focus rings from buttons, links or the search field.
Accessibility

Accessibility

  • The error heading is the first meaningful element, announced to screen readers on load.
  • Every button carries a clear text label; the search field uses aria-label=“Search our site”.
  • Decorative visuals (grid, ghost 404, split panels, illustrations) are hidden from assistive tech.
  • Visible brand focus rings on every interactive element; Tab order flows heading → actions → links.
  • All text meets WCAG AA contrast in both light and dark modes.

Email templates

Powerful and customizable email templates for modern apps and websites — ten production-ready transactional and marketing emails: welcomes, invites, verification, image, video and device-mockup layouts. Built with React Email and React Aria, styled with Tailwind CSS. Every template supports light and dark mode via a theme prop; toggle any preview below. The source’s purple brand maps to Tungo’s magenta throughout.

Example
Get the code

Email template example

The flagship pattern — Simple welcome 01: a header with nav, a short onboarding paragraph, a “Log in” CTA and a standard footer with social icons.

Tungo
HomeBlogTutorialSupport

Hi Olivia,

We’re glad to have you onboard! You’re already on your way to creating beautiful visual products.

Whether you’re here for your team(s), for a course, or just for fun—welcome! If there’s anything you need, we’ll be here every step of the way.

Log in

Thanks,
The team

This email was sent to olivia@tungo.com. If you’d rather not receive this kind of email, you can unsubscribe or manage your email preferences.

© 2077 Tungo, 100 Smith Street, Melbourne VIC 3000

These are a collection of common transactional and marketing email templates.

Get started

Installation

You can add any email template with the CLI, or copy it in manually. Templates render to HTML for any provider (SendGrid, Resend, Postmark…).

npx tungo-ui@latest add email-simple-welcome-01
// npm install @react-email/components, then
import SimpleWelcome01 from "@/emails/simple-welcome-01";
render(<SimpleWelcome01 theme="light" />);

Email template examples

Below are ten production-ready templates. Use the toggle to switch the gallery between grid and list view, or search by name. Every preview has its own Dark mode toggle and an interactive video player where shown.

Transactional / Onboarding

A header with nav links, a short onboarding paragraph, a “Log in” CTA and a standard footer. The only free template.

Tungo

Welcome to Tungo

We’re excited to welcome you to Tungo and we’re even more excited about what we’ve got planned. You’re already on your way to creating beautiful visual products.

Whether you’re here for your team(s), for a course, or just for fun—welcome! If there’s anything you need, we’ll be here every step of the way.

Tungo changelog Weekly new updates and improvements to Tungo.Follow us on X Already, hop on to date with the latest snerucs nemehrs and tips.Why we’re building Tungo Quietly Untitled is most relevant to design content and 2014.

If you have any questions, reach us a message at help@tungo.com or do si. We’d love to hear from you.

— The team

Anatomy & reference

The shared building blocks, the comparison matrix, and the rules that keep these emails consistent across clients.

Anatomy

Shared building blocks

  • Header — three variants: logo + nav links (Simple welcome 01), logo only (most), and logo + log in + social icons (Mockup 01).
  • Body — greeting, paragraph text, and an optional hero (photo, abstract image, video player or device mockup).
  • CTA button — solid magenta, rounded; some carry an icon (“Watch video” ►, “Download now” →).
  • Footer — three variants: standard (disclaimer + unsubscribe + copyright + social), minimal (social + sustainability note), and extended/legal (subscription note + Terms / Privacy / Cookies / Contact + address).
Comparison matrix

The ten templates

TemplateCTAImageVideoApp badgesNav linksFooter
Simple welcome 01Log in———✓Standard
Simple welcome 02Get startedHero photo———Standard
Simple inviteAccept the invite————Standard
Simple verificationVerify email————Standard
Image welcomeLog inAbstract———Standard
Video welcome 01Video playVideo thumb✓✓—Minimal
Video welcome 02Watch videoVideo thumb✓——Standard
Video welcome 03Watch videoVideo thumb✓——Standard
Mockup 01Download now →Laptop mockup—✓—Standard
Mockup 02App badgesPhone mockup—✓—Extended
Theming

Light & dark mode

Every template accepts a theme prop ("light" | "dark"). Light is white surface, dark text, magenta CTA; dark flips to a dark surface with light text. The source’s purple maps to magenta.

CTA button / links / brand--magenta
Email surface (light)#FFFFFF
Email surface (dark)#1A1A17
Body / muted texttertiary
App badge surface#181817
Accessibility

Accessibility & deliverability

  • A hidden preview line (the React Email Preview component) gives screen readers and inbox previews a meaningful summary.
  • CTAs and links are semantic and keyboard-operable in web clients; images carry alt text in code.
  • Hover, active and focus states render only in modern webmail (Gmail, Apple Mail, Outlook web) — never rely on them for meaning.
  • Colour contrast meets WCAG AA in both light and dark modes.
  • App Store and Google Play badges are trademarked — the neutral badge slots shown here are placeholders; drop in each vendor’s official badge per their brand guidelines.

Logo

The Tungo wordmark and its approved colour variations. Pick the version with the most contrast against its background, give it room to breathe, and never alter its colour, proportions or orientation. Five variants are provided as scalable SVG.

Variants
Get the code

Logo variants

The five approved colour treatments, each shown on the background it’s built for.

Default
Two-tone wordmark — white with the brand-colour accent.
Dark bg
White
Reversed all-white wordmark for brand and photo backgrounds.
Brand / Photo
Black
Solid black for light surfaces, documents and print.
Light bg
Branding colour
The full brand-colour wordmark for expressive placements.
Light bg
Black & branding colour
Black wordmark with the brand-colour accent retained.
Light bg
Usage
Get the code

When to use each

A quick guide to choosing the right variant.

VariantWhen to use
DefaultThe primary lockup — use on dark surfaces wherever possible.
WhiteReversed, for the brand colour, photography or any dark imagery.
BlackSingle-colour light backgrounds, printing and faxable documents.
Branding colourExpressive moments on light or neutral surfaces.
Black & branding colourLight surfaces where you want the accent but need strong contrast.
Spacing
Get the code

Clear space & minimum size

Keep a margin of clear space around the logo equal to the height of the wordmark, and never go below the minimum size.

clear space
Web · 40px
Compact · 28px
Min · 20px

Never reproduce the logo below 20px tall on screen.

Misuse
Get the code

Don’ts

Keep the logo consistent — avoid these common mistakes.

×Don’t stretch or squash
×Don’t rotate
×Don’t recolour
×Don’t add shadows or effects
×Don’t use low-contrast backgrounds
×Don’t place on busy patterns
Colour
Get the code

Logo colours

The wordmark is drawn in black, white and the brand colour only.

Brand colour (logo accent)#FB0089
Black#000000
White#FFFFFF

Design tokens

Color and type in one place, as CSS custom properties. Copy the block, paste into your global stylesheet, and reference the variables everywhere.

Export

Combined CSS

/* Tungo design tokens — color + type */
:root{
  /* brand */
  --color-primary:#FF1E9A; --color-cta:#11EE92; --color-secondary:#1D2F6F;
  --color-tertiary:#6DD6DA; --color-accent-dark:#FEFFBE; --color-accent-light:#6E651A;
  /* semantic */
  --color-success:#1FA34D; --color-warning:#F0A12E; --color-error:#F0433A; --color-info:#2BAAB0;
  /* neutrals (warm) */
  --n-0:#FFFFFF; --n-50:#F4F1EA; --n-200:#D9D4C7; --n-500:#8E887B; --n-900:#181817;
  /* surfaces (dark default) */
  --bg-canvas:#22221F; --bg-card:#181817; --bg-raised:#26261F;
  --text:#F4F1EA; --text-2:#A9A296; --text-3:#7E796E; --border:rgba(244,241,234,.12);
  /* type */
  --font-sans:'Inter',system-ui,sans-serif; --font-mono:'JetBrains Mono',monospace;
  --text-display-sm:30px; --text-xl:20px; --text-md:16px; --text-sm:14px; --text-xs:12px;
  --fw-regular:400; --fw-medium:500; --fw-semibold:600; --fw-bold:700; --fw-extrabold:800;
}

Full ramps and the Tailwind v4 @theme block are on the Colors and Typography pages.

Planned

This section is on the roadmap. The foundations it builds on are already shipped.
